item.title: EgoPoseFormer v2：提升AR/VR中自视角人体运动估计的高效方法
item.content:encoded:
EgoPoseFormer v2通过Transformer和自动标注系统提升AR/VR中人体运动估计精度。



摘要： EgoPoseFormer v2是一种用于增强AR/VR体验中自视角人体运动估计的方法，解决了由于视角限制、频繁遮挡和标注数据稀缺带来的挑战。该方法采用基于Transformer的模型实现时间一致性和空间定位，并引入自动标注系统以利用大量未标注数据进行训练。其特点包括可微分架构、身份条件查询、多视角空间优化、因果时间注意力机制，支持关键点和参数化身体表示。自动标注系统通过不确定性感知的半监督训练，将学习规模扩展至数千万帧。实验表明，该模型在EgoBody3M基准上比现有方法提升12.2%和19.4%的准确率，同时减少时间抖动，并进一步提升手腕MPJPE指标。

item.title: 基于分数扩散模型的统计收敛性分析
item.content:encoded:
研究分数扩散模型在有限样本下的统计收敛性。



摘要： 本文研究了基于分数的扩散模型在从有限样本中学习未知分布时的统计收敛性。在正向扩散过程和数据分布满足温和条件的前提下，作者推导了生成分布的有限样本误差界，以Wasserstein-p距离衡量。与以往结果不同，该理论适用于所有p≥1，并仅需数据分布具有有限矩，无需紧支撑、流形或平滑密度等假设。实验表明，模型能适应数据的内在几何结构，缓解维度灾难，其收敛速率依赖于数据的(p,q)-Wasserstein维度而非环境维度。该理论还连接了扩散模型与GANs的分析，以及最优传输中的尖锐最小最大率。

item.title: 提升野生动物重识别性能的增强训练框架
item.content:encoded:
通过增强训练提升野生动物图像重识别准确率。



摘要： 本文研究了野生动物重识别中因图像退化导致的识别性能下降问题，并提出一种基于人工多样化退化增强的训练框架。实验表明，该方法在多种动物数据集上有效提升了重识别准确率，特别是在真实退化图像上的表现提升显著。研究还首次引入了由人类专家标注的退化图像数据集，并提供了公开代码和基准，推动该领域进一步发展。

item.title: RoboCasa365：家庭移动操作的全面仿真基准
item.content:encoded:
提出RoboCasa365，用于评估通用机器人性能。



摘要： 文章介绍了RoboCasa365，这是一个基于RoboCasa平台的家庭移动操作仿真基准，包含365个日常任务和2500个多样化的厨房环境，提供了超过600小时的人类示范数据和1600小时的合成数据。该基准支持多任务学习、机器人基础模型训练和终身学习等不同问题设置的系统评估。通过在该基准上进行大量实验，研究分析了任务多样性、数据集规模和环境变化对泛化能力的影响，为未来通用机器人研究提供了新见解。

item.title: 基于Transformer与卡尔曼滤波的无人机目标跟踪架构
item.content:encoded:
提出MATA架构提升无人机目标跟踪性能。



摘要： 本文针对无人机平台上的目标跟踪问题，提出一种模块化异步跟踪架构（MATA），结合基于Transformer的跟踪器与扩展卡尔曼滤波器，集成稀疏光流的自运动补偿和目标轨迹模型。研究还引入了硬件无关的嵌入式评估协议和新的指标NT2F，用于衡量跟踪器在无外部帮助下的持续时间。实验表明，该方法在多个基准数据集上均表现出色，且在Nvidia Jetson AGX Orin上的ROS 2实现验证了其在嵌入式系统中的实时性能。

item.title: LLM代码代理与重构任务评估：CodeTaste基准研究
item.content:encoded:
LLM代码代理在重构任务中表现不足，需改进与人类开发者的对齐。



摘要： 本文研究了大型语言模型（LLM）代码代理在执行重构任务时的表现。尽管LLM可以生成有效代码，但其解决方案常存在复杂性和冗余问题。作者提出CodeTaste基准，基于开源仓库中的多文件变更数据进行重构任务评估。实验表明，LLM在详细指定重构时表现良好，但在仅提供改进区域时难以识别人类开发者的选择。通过提议-实现分解和选择最佳提议，可提升对齐效果。该研究为提升LLM代码代理与人类开发实践的一致性提供了方向。

item.title: 对抗对齐的雅可比正则化提升大语言模型鲁棒性
item.content:encoded:
AAJR方法提升LLM在多智能体系统中的鲁棒性和稳定性。



摘要： 随着大语言模型进入自主多智能体生态系统，最小最大训练变得至关重要，但高度非线性策略会导致内层最大化过程中的极端局部曲率，从而引发不稳定。传统方法通过全局雅可比边界约束来应对，但过于保守，抑制了敏感性并导致性能下降。本文提出对抗对齐雅可比正则化（AAJR），通过沿对抗上升方向控制敏感性，提高政策类的可接受范围，减少近似误差和性能损失。同时，推导出步长条件以确保优化轨迹上的平滑性和内层稳定性，为智能体鲁棒性提供结构理论支持。

item.title: 基于视觉基础模型的事件流自监督预训练方法
item.content:encoded:
提出一种自监督预训练方法提升事件流表示能力。



摘要： 本文针对不规则事件流中细粒度表示学习的挑战，提出一种基于视觉基础模型（VFMs）的自监督预训练方法。通过构建同步图像-事件数据集增强跨模态对齐，并引入结构感知的蒸馏损失以优化事件表示。实验表明该方法在下游任务中表现优异，显著优于传统方法和现有预训练技术，提升了泛化能力、数据效率与迁移性。

item.title: 大型语言模型在法语医学开放问答评估中的应用研究
item.content:encoded:
LLM在医学开放问答评估中表现受生成器影响，需优化模型适应性。



摘要： 本文研究了大型语言模型（LLMs）在法语医学开放问答（OEQA）评估中的作用，探讨其作为语义等价判断者的可行性。实验对比了封闭式、通用型及生物医学领域适配模型的表现，发现LLM的判断结果高度依赖于生成答案的模型，不同生成器之间一致性差异显著。生物医学领域适配模型和大型通用模型与专家标注的对齐度最高。此外，通过监督微调和组相对策略优化（GRPO）对小型模型进行轻量级适配，显著提升了性能并降低了生成器敏感性，即使在数据有限的情况下也有效。研究强调了在评估中考虑生成器因素的重要性，并指出经过精心适配的小型模型可在资源匮乏的医学场景中支持可扩展的评估。

item.title: AgentSelect：统一的LLM代理推荐基准与评估框架
item.content:encoded:
AgentSelect构建了首个统一的代理推荐数据集与评估体系。



摘要： 本文提出AgentSelect，一个将代理选择重新定义为基于能力配置的查询到代理推荐的基准。该基准整合了111,179个查询、107,721个可部署代理和251,103条交互记录，覆盖多种代理类型。研究发现，当前方法在长尾监督下表现脆弱，强调内容感知的能力匹配的重要性。实验表明，AgentSelect训练的模型在真实代理市场中具有良好的迁移性能，为代理生态系统提供了可复现的研究基础。

item.title: EvoPrune：一种面向多模态大语言模型的早期视觉令牌剪枝方法
item.content:encoded:
EvoPrune提升MLLM推理效率，减少视觉令牌计算成本。



摘要： 本文提出EvoPrune，一种在视觉编码阶段进行的早期视觉令牌剪枝方法，旨在提升多模态大语言模型（MLLMs）的推理效率。该方法通过基于令牌相似性、多样性和注意力重要性的分层剪枝策略，保留最具信息量的视觉令牌。实验表明，在VideoMME数据集上，EvoPrune实现了2倍的推理加速，性能损失低于1%，展示了其在延迟敏感场景中的应用潜力。

item.title: SpotIt+：一种基于等价验证的Text-to-SQL系统评估工具
item.content:encoded:
SpotIt+通过等价验证评估Text-to-SQL系统，提升差异检测效率。



摘要： SpotIt+是一款开源工具，用于通过有界等价验证评估Text-to-SQL系统。它通过主动搜索数据库实例来区分生成的SQL查询与真实查询之间的差异。为了确保生成的反例具有实际意义，研究引入了约束挖掘流程，结合规则驱动的示例数据库规范挖掘和大语言模型验证。实验结果显示，该方法能够生成更具现实意义的差异数据库，同时保持高效发现生成查询与标准查询之间未被传统测试方法发现的差异的能力。

item.title: 基于FINEST的敏感话题响应评估与改进方法
item.content:encoded:
提出FINEST评估框架提升大语言模型敏感话题响应质量。



摘要： 本文针对大语言模型在敏感话题上响应过于谨慎和模糊的问题，提出FINEST评估框架，将有用性和安全性分解为内容、逻辑和适当性三个类别。通过实验表明，基于FINEST的改进方法显著提升了模型在敏感话题上的表现，特别是评分指导的改进方式效果最佳，有效降低了适当性错误率。

item.title: 基于多智能体的生理监测系统Vivaldi在急诊护理中的评估
item.content:encoded:
多智能体系统Vivaldi在急诊护理中表现出复杂性能差异。



摘要： 文章介绍了Vivaldi，一个用于解释多变量生理时间序列的角色结构化多智能体系统。由于监管限制，研究在小规模临床试验中验证了该系统，结果显示其性能依赖于上下文，与传统假设不同。实验表明，代理管道对非思考和医学优化模型有显著提升，但对思考模型可能降低解释质量。此外，工具计算对可编码指标至关重要，而主观目标如疼痛评分变化有限。专家评估还指出，可视化方式影响临床效用，医学专用模型在效用与清晰度之间取得最佳平衡。研究强调了代理AI在选择性计算外化而非最大推理复杂性上的价值。

item.title: AI系统故障响应机制与风险缓解分类研究
item.content:encoded:
分析AI事故响应，构建系统性风险缓解分类体系。



摘要： 本文基于9,705篇媒体报道的AI事故案例，提取6,893篇中的具体应对措施，对MIT AI风险缓解分类体系进行扩展。研究引入四个新的应对类别：纠正与限制措施、法律与监管行动、金融与市场控制、规避与拒绝策略。通过标注32个标签，生成23,994条响应记录，其中67%为新发现的应对模式，显著提升原分类体系的覆盖范围和适用性。该研究强化了从问题诊断到解决方案的指导流程，并推动了部署后持续监控机制的发展，以防止系统性故障扩散。

item.title: ErrorLLM：一种用于文本到SQL生成的显式错误建模框架
item.content:encoded:
ErrorLLM通过显式建模错误提升SQL生成质量。



摘要： 本文提出ErrorLLM，一个专门用于文本到SQL生成的错误建模框架。该框架通过结构化表示用户问题和数据库模式，结合静态检测识别执行失败和表面不匹配，并利用专用错误标记扩展语义空间以捕捉隐式语义错误类型。通过精心设计的训练策略，ErrorLLM能够通过预测错误标记检测复杂隐式错误，并在检测基础上进行SQL结构的误差引导优化。实验表明，ErrorLLM在初始生成基础上实现了显著提升，且高检测F1分数保障了修正效果。

item.title: 基于零样本文本转语音的数据增强在低资源个性化语音合成中的应用
item.content:encoded:
零样本TTS数据增强提升低资源语音合成的说话人相似度。



摘要： 本文研究了将零样本文本转语音（ZS-TTS）作为数据增强手段，用于低资源个性化语音合成。尽管合成数据能提供丰富的语言和语音多样性，但直接混合大量合成语音与少量真实录音会导致说话人相似度下降。为此，作者提出ZeSTA框架，通过轻量级领域嵌入区分真实与合成语音，并结合真实数据过采样来稳定适应过程，无需修改基础架构。实验表明，该方法在LibriTTS和内部数据集上提升了说话人相似度，同时保持了可理解性和感知质量。

item.title: 基于深度学习的高b值扩散加权乳腺MRI伪影检测研究
item.content:encoded:
使用深度学习检测高b值扩散加权乳腺MRI中的伪影，效果显著。



摘要： 本研究旨在利用深度学习方法检测高b值扩散加权成像（b=1500 s/mm²）中的高信号和低信号伪影。通过回顾性分析2022年至2023年间进行的11806张3T乳腺MRI图像，训练了三种卷积神经网络模型（DenseNet121、ResNet18和SEResNet50）。其中DenseNet121在二分类任务中表现最佳，分别实现了0.92和0.94的AUROC值，用于检测高信号和低信号伪影。进一步的多类分类任务中，其加权AUROC分别为0.85和0.88。放射科医生对预测边界框的精度进行了评估，结果显示高信号伪影平均得分为3.33，低信号伪影为2.62。结果表明，基于CNN的高b值扩散加权乳腺MRI伪影检测具有潜力，但仍需进一步验证。

item.title: 基于编码器的去除非敏感依赖数据表示方法
item.content:encoded:
提出一种新方法去除数据中的非敏感依赖，提升公平性和隐私保护。



摘要： 本文提出了一种基于编码器的方法，用于从数据中移除与敏感变量相关的依赖关系，同时保留数据的核心特征。该方法不同于传统的去相关或对抗学习方法，而是通过直接优化独立性来消除统计依赖。具体而言，结合了专用变分自编码器和基于非参数最近邻密度估计的新损失函数，实现了对数据分布的显式估计与修改。实验结果显示，该方法在多个数据集上优于现有无监督技术，并能与有监督方法相媲美。

item.title: 基于稀疏门控专家框架的病理报告生成方法
item.content:encoded:
提出RANGER框架提升病理报告生成效果。



摘要： 本文针对病理报告生成任务中图像规模大、形态复杂的问题，提出RANGER框架。该框架采用稀疏门控混合专家架构，结合动态专家专业化和自适应检索重排序模块，有效减少噪声并提升语义对齐。实验表明，RANGER在PathText-BRCA数据集上表现优异，各项指标均优于现有方法，验证了其在语义驱动病理报告生成中的有效性。

item.title: 模型结构对联邦学习后门攻击的影响研究
item.content:encoded:
模型结构显著影响后门攻击效果，提出结构感知评估框架。



摘要： 本文研究了联邦学习中模型结构对后门攻击的影响。传统方法多关注触发设计或污染策略，但忽略了模型结构对扰动效果的影响。文章引入两个指标——结构响应分数（SRS）和结构兼容系数（SCC），用于衡量模型对扰动的敏感性和对分形扰动的偏好。基于此，提出了结构感知的分形扰动注入框架（TFI）。实验表明，具有多路径特征融合的网络能有效放大并保留扰动，而结构兼容性低的模型则限制了扰动效果。进一步分析显示，SCC与攻击成功率高度相关，可预测扰动的存活能力。研究揭示了模型结构与聚合机制之间的交互作用对后门行为的重要影响，为结构感知防御设计提供了新思路。

item.title: 基于互信息减少的不可学习样本生成方法
item.content:encoded:
本文提出一种新的不可学习样本生成方法，提升数据隐私保护。



摘要： 随着互联网上可自由抓取的数据量激增，深度学习取得了巨大成功，但也引发了对数据隐私和安全的关注。为防止未经授权的模型学习数据，已有许多生成不可学习样本的方法，但这些方法多依赖经验启发式策略，缺乏理论解释。本文从互信息减少的角度分析并改进不可学习样本，证明有效的不可学习样本会降低干净特征与污染特征之间的互信息，并在网络更深时表现更优。进一步从协方差减少的角度证明，最小化类内污染特征的条件协方差可以降低分布间的互信息。基于此，提出一种名为MI-UE的新方法，通过最大化类内特征的余弦相似度来减少协方差，从而有效阻碍模型泛化。实验表明该方法优于现有方法，即使在防御机制下也表现良好。

item.title: COREA：通过协作提升推理效率与成本平衡的系统
item.content:encoded:
COREA结合小模型与大模型，提升推理效率并降低成本。



摘要： 本文提出COREA系统，通过将小型语言模型（SLM）与大型语言模型（LLM）级联，实现复杂推理任务中准确性和成本之间的平衡。COREA首先由SLM尝试回答问题，并输出答案和置信度评分；若置信度低于阈值，则由LLM进行更精确的解答。研究引入基于强化学习的训练算法，通过额外的置信度校准奖励来优化SLM的置信度预测。实验表明，该方法在多个数据集和模型架构上均提升了SLM的推理能力和置信度校准效果。相比单独使用LLM，COREA在跨域数学和非数学数据集上的成本分别降低了21.5%和16.8%，且仅导致约2%的准确率下降。

item.title: ViterbiPlanNet：基于可微维特比层的程序规划框架
item.content:encoded:
ViterbiPlanNet通过可微维特比层提升程序规划效率与性能。



摘要： 本文提出ViterbiPlanNet，一种将程序知识显式整合到学习过程中的框架，利用可微维特比层（DVL）和程序知识图（PKG）实现端到端优化。该方法在CrossTask、COIN和NIV数据集上表现优异，参数量仅为扩散模型和大语言模型的十分之一，且在样本效率和鲁棒性方面有显著提升。研究还统一了测试协议，通过多次实验和自助法评估结果的统计显著性。

item.title: 单目6D姿态估计的高效框架Yolo-Key-6D
item.content:encoded:
提出一种高效的单阶段6D姿态估计方法，适用于实时应用。



摘要： 本文提出Yolo-Key-6D，一种基于YOLO的单阶段、端到端框架，用于从单张RGB图像中估计物体的6D姿态。该方法通过引入辅助头回归3D边界框角点的2D投影，提升网络对三维几何的理解。同时采用9D连续表示进行旋转回归，并通过奇异值分解映射到SO(3)空间，实现稳定训练。在LINEMOD和LINEMOD-Occluded数据集上，该方法分别取得96.24%和69.41%的准确率，且具备实时性能，展示了其在实际部署中的有效性。

item.title: 端到端全局事件重建方法提升未来对撞机实验精度
item.content:encoded:
新型事件重建方法提升Higgs粒子测量精度与效率。



摘要： 未来对撞机实验需要更高精度的Higgs、电弱和味物理可观测量，这对事件重建提出了更高要求。本文提出一种端到端的全局事件重建方法，直接将带电粒子轨迹和探测器信号映射到粒子级对象。该方法结合几何代数Transformer网络与基于目标凝聚的聚类技术，并通过专用网络进行粒子识别和能量回归。在FCC-ee的全模拟电子正电子碰撞中测试，该方法比现有规则算法提升10%-20%的重建效率，将带电强子的假粒子率降低两个数量级，并提高可见能量和不变质量分辨率22%。该框架解耦了重建性能与探测器特定调优，有助于加速未来对撞机探测器的设计迭代。

item.title: YouTube Affiliate营销透明度与合规性研究
item.content:encoded:
研究揭示YouTube affiliate营销普遍存在，但披露不合规问题严重。



摘要： 本文探讨了YouTube平台上创作者通过联盟营销获利的现象，指出尽管监管机构如美国联邦贸易委员会（FTC）已发布相关指南，但许多创作者未按规定披露关联关系，导致透明度和伦理问题。研究利用最新的网络测量和自然语言处理技术，分析了近十年200万条视频数据，发现联盟链接广泛存在，但合规率低。此外，研究还评估了不同利益相关方对提升披露行为的影响，建议监管机构、联盟合作伙伴与平台合作，以提高透明度和信任度。

item.title: 基于点云的楔形文字泥板元数据分类网络
item.content:encoded:
提出一种新型网络结构用于楔形文字泥板元数据分类。



摘要： 本文提出一种用于分类楔形文字泥板元数据的网络结构。由于现有语料库规模庞大，而专家数量有限，该任务面临挑战。文章中采用了一种受卷积启发的架构，逐步降采样点云并整合局部邻域信息，再通过特征空间中的邻域计算引入全局信息。与最先进的Point-BERT模型相比，该方法在多个指标上表现最佳。相关代码和数据集将在发表时公开。

item.title: 流匹配在强化学习中的价值函数估计研究
item.content:encoded:
流匹配提升强化学习价值估计性能。



摘要： 本文探讨了流匹配在强化学习中用于标量Q值函数估计的有效性。研究表明，其成功并非源于分布式强化学习，而是通过积分读取值和每一步的密集速度监督，增强了时序差分学习。该方法通过测试时恢复机制减少早期估计误差，并通过多点速度监督促进网络的可塑性特征学习，从而在高UTD在线RL任务中显著优于传统单体批评者，提升性能与样本效率。

item.title: 面向复杂背景的小目标检测方法研究
item.content:encoded:
提出多级特征增强与全局关系建模框架提升小目标检测性能。



摘要： 本文针对复杂背景下小目标检测中特征退化、语义表示弱和定位不准的问题，提出一种多级特征增强与全局关系建模的检测框架。该框架引入了残差哈尔小波下采样模块以保留细粒度结构信息，并采用全局关系建模模块增强语义感知和抑制背景干扰。同时设计了跨尺度混合注意力模块，实现多尺度特征的有效融合。最后结合中心辅助损失函数提升训练稳定性与定位精度。实验表明，该方法在RGBT-Tiny基准上优于现有先进方法。

item.title: LifeBench: A Benchmark for Long-Horizon Multi-Source Memory
item.content:encoded: Long-term memory is fundamental for personalized agents capable of accumulating knowledge, reasoning over user experiences, and adapting across time. However, existing memory benchmarks primarily target declarative memory, specifically semantic and episodic types, where all information is explicitly presented in dialogues. In contrast, real-world actions are also governed by non-declarative memory, including habitual and procedural types, and need to be inferred from diverse digital traces. To bridge this gap, we introduce Lifebench, which features densely connected, long-horizon event simulation. It pushes AI agents beyond simple recall, requiring the integration of declarative and non-declarative memory reasoning across diverse and temporally extended contexts. Building such a benchmark presents two key challenges: ensuring data quality and scalability. We maintain data quality by employing real-world priors, including anonymized social surveys, map APIs, and holiday-integrated calendars, thus enforcing fidelity, diversity and behavioral rationality within the dataset. Towards scalability, we draw inspiration from cognitive science and structure events according to their partonomic hierarchy; enabling efficient parallel generation while maintaining global coherence. Performance results show that top-tier, state-of-the-art memory systems reach just 55.2\% accuracy, highlighting the inherent difficulty of long-horizon retrieval and multi-source integration within our proposed benchmark. The dataset and data synthesis code are available at https://github.com/1754955896/LifeBench.

item.title: MAGE: Meta-Reinforcement Learning for Language Agents toward Strategic Exploration and Exploitation
item.content:encoded: Large Language Model (LLM) agents have demonstrated remarkable proficiency in learned tasks, yet they often struggle to adapt to non-stationary environments with feedback. While In-Context Learning and external memory offer some flexibility, they fail to internalize the adaptive ability required for long-term improvement. Meta-Reinforcement Learning (meta-RL) provides an alternative by embedding the learning process directly within the model. However, existing meta-RL approaches for LLMs focus primarily on exploration in single-agent settings, neglecting the strategic exploitation necessary for multi-agent environments. We propose MAGE, a meta-RL framework that empowers LLM agents for strategic exploration and exploitation. MAGE utilizes a multi-episode training regime where interaction histories and reflections are integrated into the context window. By using the final episode reward as the objective, MAGE incentivizes the agent to refine its strategy based on past experiences. We further combine population-based training with an agent-specific advantage normalization technique to enrich agent diversity and ensure stable learning. Experiment results show that MAGE outperforms existing baselines in both exploration and exploitation tasks. Furthermore, MAGE exhibits strong generalization to unseen opponents, suggesting it has internalized the ability for strategic exploration and exploitation. Code is available at https://github.com/Lu-Yang666/MAGE.

item.title: Data-Aware Random Feature Kernel for Transformers
item.content:encoded: Transformers excel across domains, yet their quadratic attention complexity poses a barrier to scaling. Random-feature attention, as in Performers, can reduce this cost to linear in the sequence length by approximating the softmax kernel with positive random features drawn from an isotropic distribution. In pretrained models, however, queries and keys are typically anisotropic. This induces high Monte Carlo variance in isotropic sampling schemes unless one retrains the model or uses a large feature budget. Importance sampling can address this by adapting the sampling distribution to the input geometry, but complex data-dependent proposal distributions are often intractable. We show that by data aligning the softmax kernel, we obtain an attention mechanism which can both admit a tractable minimal-variance proposal distribution for importance sampling, and exhibits better training stability. Motivated by this finding, we introduce DARKFormer, a Data-Aware Random-feature Kernel transformer that features a data-aligned kernel geometry. DARKFormer learns the random-projection covariance, efficiently realizing an importance-sampled positive random-feature estimator for its data-aligned kernel. Empirically, DARKFormer narrows the performance gap with exact softmax attention, particularly in finetuning regimes where pretrained representations are anisotropic. By combining random-feature efficiency with data-aware kernels, DARKFormer advances kernel-based attention in resource-constrained settings.

item.title: Adaptive Enhancement and Dual-Pooling Sequential Attention for Lightweight Underwater Object Detection with YOLOv10
item.content:encoded: Underwater object detection constitutes a pivotal endeavor within the realms of marine surveillance and autonomous underwater systems; however, it presents significant challenges due to pronounced visual impairments arising from phenomena such as light absorption, scattering, and diminished contrast. In response to these formidable challenges, this manuscript introduces a streamlined yet robust framework for underwater object detection, grounded in the YOLOv10 architecture. The proposed method integrates a Multi-Stage Adaptive Enhancement module to improve image quality, a Dual-Pooling Sequential Attention (DPSA) mechanism embedded into the backbone to strengthen multi-scale feature representation, and a Focal Generalized IoU Objectness (FGIoU) loss to jointly improve localization accuracy and objectness prediction under class imbalance. Comprehensive experimental evaluations conducted on the RUOD and DUO benchmark datasets substantiate that the proposed DPSA_FGIoU_YOLOv10n attains exceptional performance, achieving mean Average Precision (mAP) scores of 88.9% and 88.0% at IoU threshold 0.5, respectively. In comparison to the baseline YOLOv10n, this represents enhancements of 6.7% for RUOD and 6.2% for DUO, all while preserving a compact model architecture comprising merely 2.8M parameters. These findings validate that the proposed framework establishes an efficacious equilibrium among accuracy, robustness, and real-time operational efficiency, making it suitable for deployment in resource-constrained underwater settings.

item.title: InEdit-Bench: Benchmarking Intermediate Logical Pathways for Intelligent Image Editing Models
item.content:encoded: Multimodal generative models have made significant strides in image editing, demonstrating impressive performance on a variety of static tasks. However, their proficiency typically does not extend to complex scenarios requiring dynamic reasoning, leaving them ill-equipped to model the coherent, intermediate logical pathways that constitute a multi-step evolution from an initial state to a final one. This capacity is crucial for unlocking a deeper level of procedural and causal understanding in visual manipulation. To systematically measure this critical limitation, we introduce InEdit-Bench, the first evaluation benchmark dedicated to reasoning over intermediate pathways in image editing. InEdit-Bench comprises meticulously annotated test cases covering four fundamental task categories: state transition, dynamic process, temporal sequence, and scientific simulation. Additionally, to enable fine-grained evaluation, we propose a set of assessment criteria to evaluate the logical coherence and visual naturalness of the generated pathways, as well as the model's fidelity to specified path constraints. Our comprehensive evaluation of 14 representative image editing models on InEdit-Bench reveals significant and widespread shortcomings in this domain. By providing a standardized and challenging benchmark, we aim for InEdit-Bench to catalyze research and steer development towards more dynamic, reason-aware, and intelligent multimodal generative models.

item.title: STEM Faculty Perspectives on Generative AI in Higher Education
item.content:encoded: Generative artificial intelligence (GenAI) tools are increasingly present in higher education, yet their adoption has been largely student-driven, requiring instructors to respond to technologies already embedded in classroom practices. While some faculty have embraced GenAI for pedagogical purposes such as content generation, assessment support, and curriculum design, others approach these tools with caution, citing concerns about student learning, assessment validity, and academic integrity. Understanding faculty perspectives is therefore essential for informing effective pedagogical strategies and institutional policies. In this paper, we present findings from a focus group study with 29 STEM faculty members at a large public university in the United States. We examine how faculty integrate GenAI into their courses, the benefits and challenges they perceive for student learning, and the institutional support they identify as necessary for effective and responsible adoption. Our findings highlight key patterns in how STEM faculty engage with GenAI, reflecting both active adoption and cautious use. Faculty described a range of pedagogical applications alongside concerns about student learning, assessment, and academic integrity. Overall, the results suggest that effective integration of GenAI in higher education requires rethinking assessment, pedagogy, and institutional governance in addition to technical adoption.

item.title: ArtHOI: Articulated Human-Object Interaction Synthesis by 4D Reconstruction from Video Priors
item.content:encoded: Synthesizing physically plausible articulated human-object interactions (HOI) without 3D/4D supervision remains a fundamental challenge. While recent zero-shot approaches leverage video diffusion models to synthesize human-object interactions, they are largely confined to rigid-object manipulation and lack explicit 4D geometric reasoning. To bridge this gap, we formulate articulated HOI synthesis as a 4D reconstruction problem from monocular video priors: given only a video generated by a diffusion model, we reconstruct a full 4D articulated scene without any 3D supervision. This reconstruction-based approach treats the generated 2D video as supervision for an inverse rendering problem, recovering geometrically consistent and physically plausible 4D scenes that naturally respect contact, articulation, and temporal coherence. We introduce ArtHOI, the first zero-shot framework for articulated human-object interaction synthesis via 4D reconstruction from video priors. Our key designs are: 1) Flow-based part segmentation: leveraging optical flow as a geometric cue to disentangle dynamic from static regions in monocular video; 2) Decoupled reconstruction pipeline: joint optimization of human motion and object articulation is unstable under monocular ambiguity, so we first recover object articulation, then synthesize human motion conditioned on the reconstructed object states. ArtHOI bridges video-based generation and geometry-aware reconstruction, producing interactions that are both semantically aligned and physically grounded. Across diverse articulated scenes (e.g., opening fridges, cabinets, microwaves), ArtHOI significantly outperforms prior methods in contact accuracy, penetration reduction, and articulation fidelity, extending zero-shot interaction synthesis beyond rigid manipulation through reconstruction-informed synthesis.

item.title: CONCUR: Benchmarking LLMs for Concurrent Code Generation
item.content:encoded: Leveraging Large Language Models (LLMs) for code generation has increasingly emerged as a common practice in the domain of software engineering. Relevant benchmarks have been established to evaluate the code generation capabilities of LLMs. However, existing benchmarks focus primarily on sequential code, lacking the ability to effectively evaluate LLMs on concurrent code generation. Compared to sequential code, concurrent code exhibits greater complexity and possesses unique types of bugs, such as deadlocks and race conditions, that do not occur in sequential code. Therefore, a benchmark for evaluating sequential code generation cannot be useful for evaluating concurrent code generation with LLMs. To address this gap, we designed a benchmark CONCUR specifically aimed at evaluating the capability of LLMs to generate concurrent code. CONCUR consists of a base set of 43 concurrency problems derived from a standard concurrency textbook, together with 72 validated mutant variants, resulting in 115 total problems. The base problems serve as the semantic core of the benchmark, while the mutants expand linguistic and structural diversity. We conducted an evaluation of a range of LLMs on CONCUR, highlighting limitations of current models. Overall, our work provides a novel direction for evaluating the capability of LLMs to generate code with focus on concurrency.

item.title: Specialization of softmax attention heads: insights from the high-dimensional single-location model
item.content:encoded: Multi-head attention enables transformer models to represent multiple attention patterns simultaneously. Empirically, head specialization emerges in distinct stages during training, while many heads remain redundant and learn similar representations. We propose a theoretical model capturing this phenomenon, based on the multi-index and single-location regression frameworks. In the first part, we analyze the training dynamics of multi-head softmax attention under SGD, revealing an initial unspecialized phase followed by a multi-stage specialization phase in which different heads sequentially align with latent signal directions. In the second part, we study the impact of attention activation functions on performance. We show that softmax-1 significantly reduces noise from irrelevant heads. Finally, we introduce the Bayes-softmax attention, which achieves optimal prediction performance in this setting.

item.title: CoRe-BT: A Multimodal Radiology-Pathology-Text Benchmark for Robust Brain Tumor Typing
item.content:encoded: Accurate brain tumor typing requires integrating heterogeneous clinical evidence, including magnetic resonance imaging (MRI), histopathology, and pathology reports, which are often incomplete at the time of diagnosis. We introduce CoRe-BT, a cross-modal radiology-pathology-text benchmark for brain tumor typing, designed to study robust multimodal learning under missing modality conditions. The dataset comprises 310 patients with multi-sequence brain MRI (T1, T1c, T2, FLAIR), including 95 cases with paired H&E-stained whole-slide pathology images and pathology reports. All cases are annotated with tumor type and grade, and MRI volumes include expert-annotated tumor masks, enabling both region-aware modeling and auxiliary learning tasks. Tumors are categorized into six clinically relevant classes capturing the heterogeneity of common and rare glioma subtypes. We evaluate tumor typing under variable modality availability by comparing MRI-only models with multimodal approaches that incorporate pathology information when present. Baseline experiments demonstrate the feasibility of multimodal fusion and highlight complementary modality contributions across clinically relevant typing tasks. CoRe-BT provides a grounded testbed for advancing multimodal glioma typing and representation learning in realistic scenarios with incomplete clinical data.

item.title: Relational In-Context Learning via Synthetic Pre-training with Structural Prior
item.content:encoded: Relational Databases (RDBs) are the backbone of modern business, yet they lack foundation models comparable to those in text or vision. A key obstacle is that high-quality RDBs are private, scarce and structurally heterogeneous, making internet-scale pre-training infeasible. To overcome this data scarcity, We introduce $\textbf{RDB-PFN}$, the first relational foundation model trained purely via $\textbf{synthetic data}$. Inspired by Prior-Data Fitted Networks (PFNs) where synthetic data generated from Structural Causal Models (SCMs) enables reasoning on single tables, we design a $\textbf{Relational Prior Generator}$ to create an infinite stream of diverse RDBs from scratch. Pre-training on $\textbf{over 2 million}$ synthetic single-table and relational tasks, RDB-PFN learns to adapt to any new database instantly via genuine $\textbf{in-context learning}$. Experiments verify RDB-PFN achieves strong few-shot performance on 19 real-world relational prediction tasks, outperforming graph-based and single-table foundation-model baselines (given the same DFS-linearized inputs), while using a lightweight architecture and fast inference. The code is available at https://github.com/MuLabPKU/RDBPFN

item.title: Specification-Driven Generation and Evaluation of Discrete-Event World Models via the DEVS Formalism
item.content:encoded: World models are essential for planning and evaluation in agentic systems, yet existing approaches lie at two extremes: hand-engineered simulators that offer consistency and reproducibility but are costly to adapt, and implicit neural models that are flexible but difficult to constrain, verify, and debug over long horizons. We seek a principled middle ground that combines the reliability of explicit simulators with the flexibility of learned models, allowing world models to be adapted during online execution. By targeting a broad class of environments whose dynamics are governed by the ordering, timing, and causality of discrete events, such as queueing and service operations, embodied task planning, and message-mediated multi-agent coordination, we advocate explicit, executable discrete-event world models synthesized directly from natural-language specifications. Our approach adopts the DEVS formalism and introduces a staged LLM-based generation pipeline that separates structural inference of component interactions from component-level event and timing logic. To evaluate generated models without a unique ground truth, simulators emit structured event traces that are validated against specification-derived temporal and semantic constraints, enabling reproducible verification and localized diagnostics. Together, these contributions produce world models that are consistent over long-horizon rollouts, verifiable from observable behavior, and efficient to synthesize on demand during online execution.

item.title: CubeComposer: Spatio-Temporal Autoregressive 4K 360° Video Generation from Perspective Video
item.content:encoded: Generating high-quality 360° panoramic videos from perspective input is one of the crucial applications for virtual reality (VR), whereby high-resolution videos are especially important for immersive experience. Existing methods are constrained by computational limitations of vanilla diffusion models, only supporting $\leq$ 1K resolution native generation and relying on suboptimal post super-resolution to increase resolution. We introduce CubeComposer, a novel spatio-temporal autoregressive diffusion model that natively generates 4K-resolution 360° videos. By decomposing videos into cubemap representations with six faces, CubeComposer autoregressively synthesizes content in a well-planned spatio-temporal order, reducing memory demands while enabling high-resolution output. Specifically, to address challenges in multi-dimensional autoregression, we propose: (1) a spatio-temporal autoregressive strategy that orchestrates 360° video generation across cube faces and time windows for coherent synthesis; (2) a cube face context management mechanism, equipped with a sparse context attention design to improve efficiency; and (3) continuity-aware techniques, including cube-aware positional encoding, padding, and blending to eliminate boundary seams. Extensive experiments on benchmark datasets demonstrate that CubeComposer outperforms state-of-the-art methods in native resolution and visual quality, supporting practical VR application scenarios. Project page: https://lg-li.github.io/project/cubecomposer

item.title: Efficient Refusal Ablation in LLM through Optimal Transport
item.content:encoded: Safety-aligned language models refuse harmful requests through learned refusal behaviors encoded in their internal representations. Recent activation-based jailbreaking methods circumvent these safety mechanisms by applying orthogonal projections to remove refusal directions, but these approaches treat refusal as a one-dimensional phenomenon and ignore the rich distributional structure of model activations. We introduce a principled framework based on optimal transport theory that transforms the entire distribution of harmful activations to match harmless ones. By combining PCA with closed-form Gaussian optimal transport, we achieve efficient computation in high-dimensional representation spaces while preserving essential geometric structure. Across six models (Llama-2, Llama-3.1, Qwen-2.5; 7B-32B parameters), our method achieves up to 11% higher attack success rates than state-of-the-art baselines while maintaining comparable perplexity, demonstrating superior preservation of model capabilities. Critically, we discover that layer-selective intervention (applying optimal transport to 1-2 carefully chosen layers at approximately 40-60% network depth) substantially outperforms full-network interventions, revealing that refusal mechanisms may be localized rather than distributed. Our analysis provides new insights into the geometric structure of safety representations and suggests that current alignment methods may be vulnerable to distributional attacks beyond simple direction removal.

item.title: DeNuC: Decoupling Nuclei Detection and Classification in Histopathology
item.content:encoded: Pathology Foundation Models (FMs) have shown strong performance across a wide range of pathology image representation and diagnostic tasks. However, FMs do not exhibit the expected performance advantage over traditional specialized models in Nuclei Detection and Classification (NDC). In this work, we reveal that jointly optimizing nuclei detection and classification leads to severe representation degradation in FMs. Moreover, we identify that the substantial intrinsic disparity in task difficulty between nuclei detection and nuclei classification renders joint NDC optimization unnecessarily computationally burdensome for the detection stage. To address these challenges, we propose DeNuC, a simple yet effective method designed to break through existing bottlenecks by Decoupling Nuclei detection and Classification. DeNuC employs a lightweight model for accurate nuclei localization, subsequently leveraging a pathology FM to encode input images and query nucleus-specific features based on the detected coordinates for classification. Extensive experiments on three widely used benchmarks demonstrate that DeNuC effectively unlocks the representational potential of FMs for NDC and significantly outperforms state-of-the-art methods. Notably, DeNuC improves F1 scores by 4.2% and 3.6% (or higher) on the BRCAM2C and PUMA datasets, respectively, while using only 16% (or fewer) trainable parameters compared to other methods. Code is available at https://github.com/ZijiangY1116/DeNuC.

item.title: Training-Free Rate-Distortion-Perception Traversal With Diffusion
item.content:encoded: The rate-distortion-perception (RDP) tradeoff characterizes the fundamental limits of lossy compression by jointly considering bitrate, reconstruction fidelity, and perceptual quality. While recent neural compression methods have improved perceptual performance, they typically operate at fixed points on the RDP surface, requiring retraining to target different tradeoffs. In this work, we propose a training-free framework that leverages pre-trained diffusion models to traverse the entire RDP surface. Our approach integrates a reverse channel coding (RCC) module with a novel score-scaled probability flow ODE decoder. We theoretically prove that the proposed diffusion decoder is optimal for the distortion-perception tradeoff under AWGN observations and that the overall framework with the RCC module achieves the optimal RDP function in the Gaussian case. Empirical results across multiple datasets demonstrate the framework's flexibility and effectiveness in navigating the ternary RDP tradeoff using pre-trained diffusion models. Our results establish a practical and theoretically grounded approach to adaptive, perception-aware compression.

item.title: A Rubric-Supervised Critic from Sparse Real-World Outcomes
item.content:encoded: Academic benchmarks for coding agents tend to reward autonomous task completion, measured by verifiable rewards such as unit-test success. In contrast, real-world coding agents operate with humans in the loop, where success signals are typically noisy, delayed, and sparse. How can we bridge this gap? In this paper, we propose a process to learn a "critic" model from sparse and noisy interaction data, which can then be used both as a reward model for either RL-based training or inference-time scaling. Specifically, we introduce Critic Rubrics, a rubric-based supervision framework with 24 behavioral features that can be derived from human-agent interaction traces alone. Using a semi-supervised objective, we can then jointly predict these rubrics and sparse human feedback (when present). In experiments, we demonstrate that, despite being trained primarily from trace-observable rubrics and sparse real-world outcome proxies, these critics improve best-of-N reranking on SWE-bench (Best@8 +15.9 over Random@8 over the rerankable subset of trajectories), enable early stopping (+17.7 with 83% fewer attempts), and support training-time data curation via critic-selected trajectories.

item.title: Bielik-Q2-Sharp: A Comparative Study of Extreme 2-bit Quantization Methods for a Polish 11B Language Model
item.content:encoded: We present Bielik-Q2-Sharp, the first systematic academic evaluation of extreme 2-bit quantization applied to a Polish large language model. Using Bielik-11B-v2.3-Instruct (11B parameters, Mistral architecture) as our base model, we compare six state-of-the-art post-training quantization methods -- QuIP#, SpinQuant+GPTQ, ButterflyQuant, QTIP, VPTQ, and AQLM -- all calibrated on a Polish-language corpus (CulturaX-PL) with shared Hessian matrices. Our best variant (QuIP# E8P12) achieves 71.92% across 22 Polish benchmarks versus 72.07% for the IQ2_XXS baseline -- within statistical noise, at a modest size premium (3.26 GB vs. ~2.6 GB). On eq_bench, our method scores 47.14 versus 43.53 (+3.6pp), suggesting superior preservation of higher-order reasoning. QTIP achieves the best per-bit efficiency (79.4% MC acc_norm at ~2.4 bpw, 3.27 GB), matching VPTQ's quality at 35% smaller size. We additionally document a MC-generation dissociation phenomenon where rotation-based methods preserve log-likelihood quality but fail catastrophically at autoregressive generation. The entire project was conducted by a single independent researcher on cloud GPUs (vast.ai) within a $285 budget. All models, Hessians, and evaluation logs are publicly available.

item.title: QD-PCQA: Quality-Aware Domain Adaptation for Point Cloud Quality Assessment
item.content:encoded: No-Reference Point Cloud Quality Assessment (NR-PCQA) still struggles with generalization, primarily due to the scarcity of annotated point cloud datasets. Since the Human Visual System (HVS) drives perceptual quality assessment independently of media types, prior knowledge on quality learned from images can be repurposed for point clouds. This insight motivates adopting Unsupervised Domain Adaptation (UDA) to transfer quality-relevant priors from labeled images to unlabeled point clouds. However, existing UDA-based PCQA methods often overlook key characteristics of perceptual quality, such as sensitivity to quality ranking and quality-aware feature alignment, thereby limiting their effectiveness. To address these issues, we propose a novel Quality-aware Domain adaptation framework for PCQA, termed QD-PCQA. The framework comprises two main components: i) a Rank-weighted Conditional Alignment (RCA) strategy that aligns features under consistent quality levels and adaptively emphasizes misranked samples to reinforce perceptual quality ranking awareness; and ii) a Quality-guided Feature Augmentation (QFA) strategy, which includes quality-guided style mixup, multi-layer extension, and dual-domain augmentation modules to augment perceptual feature alignment. Extensive cross-domain experiments demonstrate that QD-PCQA significantly improves generalization in NR-PCQA tasks. The code is available at https://github.com/huhu-code/QD-PCQA.

item.title: Field imaging framework for morphological characterization of aggregates with computer vision: Algorithms and applications
item.content:encoded: Construction aggregates, including sand and gravel, crushed stone and riprap, are the core building blocks of the construction industry. State-of-the-practice characterization methods mainly relies on visual inspection and manual measurement. State-of-the-art aggregate imaging methods have limitations that are only applicable to regular-sized aggregates under well-controlled conditions. This dissertation addresses these major challenges by developing a field imaging framework for the morphological characterization of aggregates as a multi-scenario solution. For individual and non-overlapping aggregates, a field imaging system was designed and the associated segmentation and volume estimation algorithms were developed. For 2D image analyses of aggregates in stockpiles, an automated 2D instance segmentation and morphological analysis approach was established. For 3D point cloud analyses of aggregate stockpiles, an integrated 3D Reconstruction-Segmentation-Completion (RSC-3D) approach was established: 3D reconstruction procedures from multi-view images, 3D stockpile instance segmentation, and 3D shape completion to predict the unseen sides. First, a 3D reconstruction procedure was developed to obtain high-fidelity 3D models of collected aggregate samples, based on which a 3D aggregate particle library was constructed. Next, two datasets were derived from the 3D particle library for 3D learning: a synthetic dataset of aggregate stockpiles with ground-truth instance labels, and a dataset of partial-complete shape pairs, developed with varying-view raycasting schemes. A state-of-the-art 3D instance segmentation network and a 3D shape completion network were trained on the datasets, respectively. The application of the integrated approach was demonstrated on real stockpiles and validated with ground-truth, showing good performance in capturing and predicting the unseen sides of aggregates.

item.title: 基于深度学习的肾小球病理分析模型GloPath
item.content:encoded:
GloPath在肾小球病变评估和临床病理关联研究中表现优异。



摘要： 文章介绍了GloPath，一种基于超过一百万张肾小球图像训练的实体中心基础模型，用于解决肾病诊断中的小球形态异质性和细微病变识别难题。该模型在三个独立队列的52项任务中表现出色，尤其在42项任务中超越了现有方法。此外，GloPath在真实世界研究中实现了91.51%的ROC-AUC，展示了其在临床环境中的强大鲁棒性。同时，它揭示了224对形态学参数与临床指标之间的统计显著关联，有助于将组织病理学与患者预后联系起来，为可临床转化的AI应用提供了新方向。

item.title: 基于类型感知检索增强生成的工业优化建模方法
item.content:encoded:
提出一种类型感知RAG方法提升工业优化模型可执行性。



摘要： 本文针对工业优化建模中自然语言到求解器代码的翻译问题，提出一种类型感知的检索增强生成（RAG）方法。该方法通过构建领域特定的类型知识库，结合知识图谱中的数学依赖关系，确保生成的模型具备可执行性。在电池生产的需求响应优化和柔性作业车间调度两个工业案例中验证了该方法的有效性，结果表明其能够生成可编译模型并实现最优解，而传统RAG方法则失败。消融实验进一步证明类型感知依赖闭包对避免结构幻觉和确保可执行性的关键作用。

item.title: Bengali长时语音识别与说话人分割的多阶段方法研究
item.content:encoded:
针对孟加拉语长时语音任务，提出多阶段解决方案并取得良好效果。



摘要： 本文针对孟加拉语在语音技术中的低资源问题，特别是在长时语音转录和说话人分割方面，提出了一种多阶段方法。该方法基于Kaggle上的DL Sprint 4.0竞赛，采用微调后的Whisper Medium模型进行语音转录，并结合自定义训练的分割模型与pyannote库实现说话人分割。通过两步法和超参数优化，取得了0.27的DER和0.38的WER，展示了针对南亚语言的AI包容性提升潜力。相关代码已开源。

item.title: 多数据集因果发现方法I-CAM-UV的提出与应用
item.content:encoded:
提出I-CAM-UV方法整合多数据集因果信息，提升因果关系识别效果。



摘要： 本文研究从观测数据中进行因果发现的问题，针对多数据集且变量不完全一致的情况，提出一种新的方法I-CAM-UV。该方法基于Causal Additive Models with Unobserved Variables（CAM-UV），利用各数据集中与未观测变量相关的信息，通过枚举所有结构一致的因果图来整合结果。研究展示了I-CAM-UV在处理复杂因果关系方面的有效性，并提供了一种高效的组合搜索算法，优于现有方法。

item.title: 基于曲率统计的多尺度聚类选择方法ElbowSig
item.content:encoded:
提出ElbowSig方法，用于多尺度聚类选择。



摘要： 文章提出了一种名为ElbowSig的框架，将传统的‘肘部’方法转化为严格的统计推断问题。该方法通过计算聚类异质性序列的归一化离散曲率统计量，并与无结构数据的零分布进行比较，从而识别出具有统计意义的聚类结构。研究分析了该统计量在大样本和高维情况下的渐近性质，并证明其在多种聚类方法中均适用。实验表明，ElbowSig能够在保持适当第一类错误控制的同时，有效揭示多尺度组织结构，优于单一分辨率的选择标准。

item.title: 可逆行为学习与模型适应的结构不可逆性研究
item.content:encoded:
研究模型适应中的结构不可逆性，提出可逆行为学习方法。



摘要： 本文探讨了神经网络模型在参数调整过程中的结构不可逆性问题。传统方法如微调、对齐训练和强化学习虽然在短期优化中有效，但会导致模型长期行为的改变。文章引入了结构不可逆性的概念，指出任务目标与模型表征身份的交织会导致模型行为偏离原始状态。为解决此问题，作者提出了可逆行为学习方法，使模型行为能通过显式卸载过程恢复。同时，定义了可恢复性因子作为行为可恢复性的度量，并通过实验验证了可逆适应在回滚上的有效性。

item.title: 基于多模态项目反应理论的模型评估方法
item.content:encoded:
提出M3IRT框架提升多模态模型评估可靠性。



摘要： 本文提出一种多模态和多维项目反应理论框架（M3IRT），用于评估多模态大语言模型的跨模态推理能力。该框架通过分解模型能力和题目难度的图像、文本及跨模态成分，能够识别真正需要多模态整合的问题，并在包含低质量题目的情况下仍保持排名准确性。实验表明，M3IRT能有效减少评估成本并提高基准测试的可靠性，为多模态模型评估提供实用工具。

item.title: 基于SFT与RL的持续GUI学习框架CGL
item.content:encoded:
提出CGL框架，平衡GUI任务适应与技能保留。



摘要： 本文针对GUI应用频繁更新带来的持续学习挑战，提出一种结合监督微调（SFT）和强化学习（RL）的持续GUI学习框架CGL。该框架通过动态调整SFT比例并引入梯度手术策略，有效缓解知识覆盖问题，同时提升模型对旧任务的保留能力。研究还构建了AndroidControl-CL基准，用于评估持续GUI学习性能。实验结果表明CGL在多个场景中表现优异，相关代码和模型将公开。

item.title: 大型语言模型推理阶段碳排放评估框架研究
item.content:encoded:
研究提出LLM推理阶段碳排放评估框架，提升可持续性决策能力。



摘要： 本文探讨了大型语言模型（LLM）在软件工程中的广泛应用及其带来的碳排放问题。尽管训练阶段的碳排放较高，但推理阶段由于处理大量请求，其碳排放迅速超过训练阶段。因此，准确测量推理阶段的碳排放成为实现可持续发展的关键。文章提出了一个基于提示级别的碳排放评估参考框架，为未来工具设计提供指导，并介绍了SEAL系统，该系统通过多基准方法进行每条提示的碳排放估算，初步验证结果表明其具有标准化可持续性评估的潜力。

item.title: 基于多模态的3D语义占据预测框架VLMFusionOcc3D
item.content:encoded:
提出VLMFusionOcc3D提升自动驾驶中3D语义占据预测性能。



摘要： 本文提出VLMFusionOcc3D，一个用于自动驾驶中密集3D语义占据预测的多模态框架。针对现有基于体素的占据模型在稀疏几何网格中的语义模糊性和恶劣天气下的性能下降问题，该框架利用视觉-语言模型的语言先验，将模糊的体素特征锚定到稳定的语义概念。通过双分支特征提取管道将多视角图像和LiDAR点云投影到统一的体素空间，并引入InstVLM模块注入高层语义和地理先验。同时，WeathFusion模块根据实时环境可靠性动态调整传感器贡献权重，DAGA损失确保结构一致性。实验表明，该方法在nuScenes和SemanticKITTI数据集上显著提升基线模型性能，尤其在恶劣天气下表现优异。

item.title: 基于LLM的智能体评估框架PAE及其在tau-bench上的应用分析
item.content:encoded:
PAE框架揭示LLM智能体任务执行中的多维失败模式。



摘要： 本文提出Procedure-Aware Evaluation (PAE) 框架，用于更全面地评估基于大语言模型的智能体。PAE通过结构化观察和一致性关系分析，从效用、效率、交互质量、程序完整性等维度进行评估，并引入多维门控机制以排除无效结果。在tau-bench上测试表明，27-78%的已报告成功案例存在缺陷，且不同模型表现出不同的失败特征。此外，研究还发现基准设计中存在任务范围不足、奖励信号矛盾等问题。

item.title: 基于双域协同机制的透明物体实例分割方法SEP-YOLO
item.content:encoded:
提出SEP-YOLO解决透明物体分割难题，提升分割精度。



摘要： 透明物体实例分割在计算机视觉中面临边界模糊、对比度低和依赖背景等挑战。现有方法因依赖强外观线索和清晰边界而效果不佳。本文提出SEP-YOLO框架，结合双域协同机制，引入频率域细节增强模块与多尺度空间细化流，提升边界识别与特征对齐能力。同时提供高质量的实例级标注数据，填补数据空白。实验表明，SEP-YOLO在Trans10K和GVD数据集上取得最佳性能。

item.title: CAWM-Mamba：多模态图像融合与复杂天气恢复框架
item.content:encoded:
提出CAWM-Mamba解决多天气叠加下的图像融合问题。



摘要： 本文提出CAWM-Mamba，一个用于多模态图像融合和复合天气恢复的端到端框架。该框架包含三个核心模块：天气感知预处理模块（WAPM）、跨模态特征交互模块（CFIM）以及小波域状态块（WSSB）。WSSB通过小波分解解耦多频段退化，并引入Freq-SSM模型无冗余地建模高频率退化。实验表明，CAWM-Mamba在多种数据集上优于现有方法，且在语义分割和目标检测等下游任务中表现优异，具有实际应用价值。

item.title: 基于AIGC的儿童姿势估计合成数据管道
item.content:encoded:
Synthetic-Child生成高质量儿童姿势图像，提升AI学习助手精度。



摘要： 本文提出Synthetic-Child，一种基于AIGC的合成数据生成管道，用于生成具有真实感的儿童姿势训练图像及关键点标注，无需真实儿童照片。该方法包括四个阶段：使用SMPL-X生成多样化的坐姿；通过双ControlNet生成12,000张图像；利用ViTPose进行置信度过滤；最终在RTMPose-M上微调并量化部署。实验显示，在真实数据集上，模型达到71.2 AP，比基线提升12.5 AP，且在边缘设备上运行稳定，具备实际应用潜力。

item.title: 基于视频与扫描的可动物体高精度重建与分割方法
item.content:encoded:
提出AiM框架实现无需先验知识的可动物体高质量分割与重建。



摘要： 本文提出一种名为Articulation in Motion (AiM)的新框架，用于从用户与物体的交互视频和初始状态扫描中重建可动物体的3D数字模型，并进行部件分割和关节分析。该方法通过双高斯场景表示学习物体的运动信息，利用运动线索进行部件分割和关节分配。随后采用鲁棒的顺序RANSAC算法，在无任何部件结构先验的情况下，对部件运动性进行分析，自动确定部件数量并估计运动学特性。实验表明，该方法在复杂和简单物体上均表现出色，具有较强的泛化能力。

item.title: 轻量级生成式图像压缩方法ProGIC提升传输效率与质量
item.content:encoded:
ProGIC通过残差向量量化实现高效图像压缩与渐进传输。



摘要： 本文提出一种名为ProGIC的轻量级生成式图像压缩方法，采用残差向量量化（RVQ）技术，实现从粗到细的重建和渐进式比特流。该方法结合深度可分离卷积和小注意力块，提升了在低比特率场景下的实用性。实验表明，ProGIC在DISTS和LPIPS指标上相比MS-ILLM分别提升57.57%和58.83%，同时在GPU上编码解码速度提升10倍以上，具有良好的灵活性和效率。

item.title: 量子神经网络中数据表示的几何灵活性研究
item.content:encoded:
量子神经网络需依赖可调几何变形提升特征学习能力。



摘要： 本文探讨了量子神经网络（QNN）在深度与状态可达性之外的特征学习能力问题。通过将数据视为复射影空间中的嵌入流形，分析了单位变换的李代数方向，提出CLAs映射和aCLS标准，揭示了数据独立的可训练单元与纯数据编码的局限性。研究指出，几何灵活性需要数据与可训练权重的非平凡联合依赖，并证明高维纠缠方向对多量子态流形的自适应控制至关重要。数值实验表明，满足CLS条件的数据重上传模型性能更优且计算量更低。该研究重新定义了QNN设计的核心目标为可控的隐含量子表示几何。

item.title: EvoSkill：通过迭代失败分析自动发现和优化代理技能
item.content:encoded:
EvoSkill通过分析执行失败自动优化代理技能，提升任务准确率。



摘要： 本文介绍了EvoSkill，一个自进化的框架，用于自动发现和优化代理技能。该框架通过分析执行失败，提出新的技能或修改现有技能，并将其结构化为可重用的技能文件夹。EvoSkill利用帕累托前沿选择仅提高验证性能的技能，同时保持底层模型不变。在OfficeQA和SealQA两个基准测试中，EvoSkill分别提升了7.3%和12.1%的准确率。此外，研究还表明，从一个任务中进化出的技能可以零样本迁移至其他任务，如从SealQA迁移到BrowseComp，提升5.3%的准确率，证明了技能级优化带来的可迁移能力。

item.title: 大型视觉语言模型在图结构理解中的表示分析
item.content:encoded:
LVLMs在图结构关系理解上存在困难，因边信息未被线性编码。



摘要： 本文研究了大型视觉语言模型（LVLMs）在图结构理解任务中的表现，发现其在处理节点和有向边（如箭头和线条）之间的关系时存在局限。通过构建基于有向图的合成数据集进行探针实验，发现边信息在视觉编码器中并非线性可分，仅在语言模型的文本标记中实现线性编码，而节点信息和全局结构特征已在视觉编码器的隐藏状态中线性编码。这表明不同类型的视觉信息形成线性可分表示的阶段不同，边信息的延迟编码可能解释了LVLMs在关系理解上的不足。

item.title: AI空间物理：开放自扩展智能体的构成语义
item.content:encoded:
本文提出AI空间物理概念，用于描述开放自扩展AI机构的边界行为。



摘要： 本文探讨了代理型AI系统逐渐演变为持久性机构的现象，这些系统能够积累状态、调用外部工具并扩展其未来权限范围。传统治理语言主要关注决策层约束，但对边界跨越的因果机制缺乏明确界定。为此，本文引入AI空间物理作为开放自扩展AI机构的构成语义，定义了包含类型化边界通道、有限视野范围语义和膜见证机制的最小状态模型。核心定律要求见证完整性、不可绕过中介、原子化裁决到执行的过渡以及可重放的裁决类重建。文章强调将二阶效应区分为结构扩展与政策扩展，并将扩展过渡视为治理相关事件，即使没有立即的外部变化。

item.title: 基于双层Mixture-of-Experts的统一图像修复框架
item.content:encoded:
提出一种统一图像修复框架，提升多种退化类型的处理效果。



摘要： 本文针对不同退化类型（如雾霾、模糊、噪声和低光）对图像修复带来的挑战，提出了一种基于双层Mixture-of-Experts（MoE）架构与预训练扩散模型的统一图像修复框架。该框架包含Inter-MoE层和Intra-MoE层，前者自适应组合专家组以处理主要退化类型，后者进一步选择专业子专家以应对同一类型内的细粒度变化。这种设计使模型在多样化退化类别上具备粗粒度适应能力，同时在特定类别内实现精细调节，从而有效应对复杂现实中的图像退化问题。实验表明，该方法在多个图像修复任务中优于现有技术。

item.title: 人工智能辅助诊断妊娠滋养细胞疾病
item.content:encoded:
AI模型GTDoctor提升妊娠滋养细胞疾病病理诊断准确率与效率。



摘要： 文章介绍了一种名为GTDoctor的人工智能病理诊断模型，用于提高妊娠滋养细胞疾病（GTD）的诊断效率和准确性。该模型通过像素级病灶分割技术，输出诊断结论和个性化分析结果。基于此开发的GTDiagnosis系统在临床试验中表现出色，回顾性研究显示其病灶检测平均精度超过0.91，前瞻性研究中病理医生使用该系统后阳性预测值达95.59%，并显著缩短了诊断时间。GTDoctor和GTDiagnosis为GTD病理诊断提供了新的解决方案，提升了诊断性能和效率，同时保持了临床可解释性。

item.title: 深度LDA的隐式正则化理论分析
item.content:encoded:
研究深度LDA的隐式正则化特性及其优化几何。



摘要： 本文首次对深度LDA这一旨在最小化类内方差并最大化类间距离的尺度不变目标函数所诱导的隐式正则化进行了理论分析。通过分析在L层对角线性网络上的损失梯度流，作者证明了在平衡初始化下，网络结构将标准的加法梯度更新转换为乘法权重更新，从而自动保持(2/L)准范数的守恒。

item.title: 利用归一化流降低格点场论可观测量的方差
item.content:encoded:
归一化流有效降低格点场论中可观测量的方差。



摘要： 该研究利用归一化流方法，构建了用于格点场论可观测量的无偏、低方差估计器。该方法应用于SU(3)杨-米尔斯理论和两味量子色动力学中的胶子算符插入可观测量，在胶球关联函数和与强子结构相关的胶子矩阵元中实现了10到60倍的方差降低，并展示了计算优势。方差减少效果与格点体积无关，因此可通过体积转移来降低训练成本。

item.title: 基于外生状态的强化学习算法优化研究
item.content:encoded:
利用外生状态结构提升强化学习性能。



摘要： 本文研究了一类具有外生状态组件的马尔可夫决策过程，其中这些状态的转移与智能体的动作无关。通过利用这一结构，作者证明了可以显著改善学习保证，且仅外生状态空间的大小影响最优遗憾界。同时，研究还建立了匹配的下界，表明该依赖关系在信息论上是最佳的。实验结果表明，与传统方法相比，该方法在样本效率上有显著提升。

item.title: HDINO：一种高效的开放词汇目标检测方法
item.content:encoded:
HDINO无需人工标注数据，提升开放词汇目标检测性能。



摘要： 本文提出HDINO，一种高效且简洁的开放词汇目标检测方法。该方法摒弃了传统依赖人工标注数据和复杂特征提取的模式，采用两阶段训练策略。第一阶段通过引入噪声样本构建视觉与文本模态间的语义对齐机制，并设计难度加权分类损失以提升模型表现；第二阶段通过轻量级特征融合模块增强对语言语义的敏感性。在Swin Transformer-T设置下，HDINO-T在COCO数据集上取得49.2 mAP，优于现有方法，且在微调后进一步提升至56.4 mAP。实验表明该方法有效且可扩展。

item.title: 低次多项式方法在高维统计问题中的局限性
item.content:encoded:
低次多项式方法无法预测某些可解问题的计算可行性。



摘要： 本文探讨了低次多项式框架在高维统计问题中的应用，发现其在某些情况下无法准确预测算法的计算可行性。研究提出一个在R^n中可多项式时间求解的问题，但低次多项式方法未能捕捉到其可解性，甚至在高阶度下仍失效。尽管低次矩匹配至特定阶数，但该问题属于稳健子空间恢复的特殊情况。研究结果表明，基于反集中性质的算法可能突破低次多项式方法的预测范围，挑战其作为计算障碍预测工具的普遍性。

item.title: 基于HBSN的形状表示与深度学习应用
item.content:encoded:
HBSN网络高效提取形状先验信息，提升分割模型性能。



摘要： 本文提出了一种名为Harmonic Beltrami Signature Network (HBSN)的深度学习架构，用于从二值图像中计算Harmonic Beltrami Signature (HBS)。HBS是一种具有平移、缩放和旋转不变性的二维简单连通形状表示。HBSN通过神经网络的函数逼近能力，实现了形状先验信息的高效提取与利用。该网络包含预空间变换网络（pre-STN）用于形状归一化，基于UNet的主干网络用于HBS预测，以及后置空间变换网络（post-STN）用于角度正则化。实验表明，HBSN能够准确计算复杂形状的HBS表示，并可直接嵌入现有分割模型中，提升其性能。结果验证了HBSN作为将几何形状信息嵌入计算机视觉流程的通用模块的实用性。

item.title: NeighborMAE：利用邻近图像提升地球观测图像自监督学习
item.content:encoded:
NeighborMAE通过联合重建邻近图像提升Earth Observation的自监督学习效果。



摘要： 本文提出NeighborMAE，一种基于邻近地球观测图像联合重建的自监督学习方法，以捕捉图像间的空间依赖关系。传统方法多关注多模态和多时相数据，但忽略了相邻图像之间的高度相关性。NeighborMAE通过动态调整掩码比例和像素级损失权重，有效提升了模型性能。实验表明，该方法在多个预训练数据集和下游任务中均优于现有基线，验证了邻近图像在Earth Observation中的重要价值及设计的有效性。

item.title: PVT-GDLA：高效医学图像分割的线性注意力模型
item.content:encoded:
PVT-GDLA实现高效且高精度的医学图像分割。



摘要： 本文提出一种名为PVT-GDLA的解码器中心Transformer模型，用于医学图像分割。该模型通过引入Gated Differential Linear Attention（GDLA）机制，在保持线性时间复杂度的同时，提升长距离依赖关系的捕捉能力。GDLA通过两个互补的查询/键子空间计算注意力路径，并利用可学习的通道级缩放进行差异处理，以减少噪声并增强上下文信息。同时，轻量级门控机制和深度卷积的局部token混合分支提升了边界保真度。实验表明，PVT-GDLA在CT、MRI、超声和皮肤镜等多模态医学影像数据集上均取得最优性能，参数量与FLOPs均优于现有方法，适用于临床部署。

item.title: LLandMark: A Multi-Agent Framework for Landmark-Aware Multimodal Interactive Video Retrieval
item.content:encoded: The increasing diversity and scale of video data demand retrieval systems capable of multimodal understanding, adaptive reasoning, and domain-specific knowledge integration. This paper presents LLandMark, a modular multi-agent framework for landmark-aware multimodal video retrieval to handle real-world complex queries. The framework features specialized agents that collaborate across four stages: query parsing and planning, landmark reasoning, multimodal retrieval, and reranked answer synthesis. A key component, the Landmark Knowledge Agent, detects cultural or spatial landmarks and reformulates them into descriptive visual prompts, enhancing CLIP-based semantic matching for Vietnamese scenes. To expand capabilities, we introduce an LLM-assisted image-to-image pipeline, where a large language model (Gemini 2.5 Flash) autonomously detects landmarks, generates image search queries, retrieves representative images, and performs CLIP-based visual similarity matching, removing the need for manual image input. In addition, an OCR refinement module leveraging Gemini and LlamaIndex improves Vietnamese text recognition. Experimental results show that LLandMark achieves adaptive, culturally grounded, and explainable retrieval performance.

item.title: Utonia: Toward One Encoder for All Point Clouds
item.content:encoded: We dream of a future where point clouds from all domains can come together to shape a single model that benefits them all. Toward this goal, we present Utonia, a first step toward training a single self-supervised point transformer encoder across diverse domains, spanning remote sensing, outdoor LiDAR, indoor RGB-D sequences, object-centric CAD models, and point clouds lifted from RGB-only videos. Despite their distinct sensing geometries, densities, and priors, Utonia learns a consistent representation space that transfers across domains. This unification improves perception capability while revealing intriguing emergent behaviors that arise only when domains are trained jointly. Beyond perception, we observe that Utonia representations can also benefit embodied and multimodal reasoning: conditioning vision-language-action policies on Utonia features improves robotic manipulation, and integrating them into vision-language models yields gains on spatial reasoning. We hope Utonia can serve as a step toward foundation models for sparse 3D data, and support downstream applications in AR/VR, robotics, and autonomous driving.

item.title: CAPT: Confusion-Aware Prompt Tuning for Reducing Vision-Language Misalignment
item.content:encoded: Vision-language models like CLIP have achieved remarkable progress in cross-modal representation learning, yet suffer from systematic misclassifications among visually and semantically similar categories. We observe that such confusion patterns are not random but persistently occur between specific category pairs, revealing the model's intrinsic bias and limited fine-grained discriminative ability. To address this, we propose CAPT, a Confusion-Aware Prompt Tuning framework that enables models to learn from their own misalignment. Specifically, we construct a Confusion Bank to explicitly model stable confusion relationships across categories and misclassified samples. On this basis, we introduce a Semantic Confusion Miner (SEM) to capture global inter-class confusion through semantic difference and commonality prompts, and a Sample Confusion Miner (SAM) to retrieve representative misclassified instances from the bank and capture sample-level cues through a Diff-Manner Adapter that integrates global and local contexts. To further unify confusion information across different granularities, a Multi-Granularity Difference Expert (MGDE) module is designed to jointly leverage semantic- and sample-level experts for more robust confusion-aware reasoning. Extensive experiments on 11 benchmark datasets demonstrate that our method significantly reduces confusion-induced errors while enhancing the discriminability and generalization of both base and novel classes, successfully resolving 50.72 percent of confusable sample pairs. Code will be released at https://github.com/greatest-gourmet/CAPT.

item.title: ACE-Merging: Data-Free Model Merging with Adaptive Covariance Estimation
item.content:encoded: Model merging aims to combine multiple task-specific expert models into a single model while preserving generalization across diverse tasks. However, interference among experts, especially when they are trained on different objectives, often leads to significant performance degradation. Despite recent progress, resolving this interference without data access, retraining, or architectural modification remains a fundamental challenge. This paper provides a theoretical analysis demonstrating that the input covariance of each task, which is a key factor for optimal merging, can be implicitly estimated from the parameter differences of its fine-tuned model, even in a fully data-free setting. Building on this insight, we introduce \acem, an Adaptive Covariance Estimation framework that effectively mitigates inter-task interference. Our approach features a principled, closed-form solution that contrasts with prior iterative or heuristic methods. Extensive experiments on both vision and language benchmarks demonstrate that \acem sets a new state-of-the-art among data-free methods. It consistently outperforms existing baselines; for example, \acem achieves an average absolute improvement of 4\% over the previous methods across seven tasks on GPT-2. Owing to its efficient closed-form formulation, \acem delivers superior performance with a modest computational cost, providing a practical and theoretically grounded solution for model merging.

item.title: Think-as-You-See: Streaming Chain-of-Thought Reasoning for Large Vision-Language Models
item.content:encoded: Large Vision Language Models (LVLMs) exhibit strong Chain-of-Thought (CoT) capabilities, yet most existing paradigms assume full-video availability before inference, a batch-style process misaligned with real-world video streams where information arrives sequentially. Motivated by the streaming nature of video data, we investigate two streaming reasoning paradigms for LVLMs. The first, an interleaved paradigm, alternates between receiving frames and producing partial reasoning but remains constrained by strictly ordered cache updates. To better match streaming inputs, we propose \textbf{Think-as-You-See (TaYS)}, a unified framework enabling true concurrent reasoning. TaYS integrates parallelized CoT generation, stream-constrained training, and stream-parallel inference. It further employs temporally aligned reasoning units, streaming attention masks and positional encodings, and a dual KV-cache that decouples visual encoding from textual reasoning. We evaluate all paradigms on the Qwen2.5-VL family across representative video CoT tasks, including event dynamics analysis, causal reasoning, and thematic understanding. Experiments show that TaYS consistently outperforms both batch and interleaved baselines, improving reasoning performance while substantially reducing time-to-first-token (TTFT) and overall reasoning delay. These results demonstrate the effectiveness of data-aligned streaming reasoning in enabling efficient and responsive video understanding for LVLMs. We release our code at \href{https://github.com/EIT-NLP/StreamingLLM/tree/main/TaYS}{this repository.}

item.title: TenExp: Mixture-of-Experts-Based Tensor Decomposition Structure Search Framework
item.content:encoded: Recently, tensor decompositions continue to emerge and receive increasing attention. Selecting a suitable tensor decomposition to exactly capture the low-rank structures behind the data is at the heart of the tensor decomposition field, which remains a challenging and relatively under-explored problem. Current tensor decomposition structure search methods are still confined by a fixed factor-interaction family (e.g., tensor contraction) and cannot deliver the mixture of decompositions. To address this problem, we elaborately design a mixture-of-experts-based tensor decomposition structure search framework (termed as TenExp), which allows us to dynamically select and activate suitable tensor decompositions in an unsupervised fashion. This framework enjoys two unique advantages over the state-of-the-art tensor decomposition structure search methods. Firstly, TenExp can provide a suitable single decomposition beyond a fixed factor-interaction family. Secondly, TenExp can deliver a suitable mixture of decompositions beyond a single decomposition. Theoretically, we also provide the approximation error bound of TenExp, which reveals the approximation capability of TenExp. Extensive experiments on both synthetic and realistic datasets demonstrate the superiority of the proposed TenExp compared to the state-of-the-art tensor decomposition-based methods.

item.title: MaBERT:A Padding Safe Interleaved Transformer Mamba Hybrid Encoder for Efficient Extended Context Masked Language Modeling
item.content:encoded: Self attention encoders such as Bidirectional Encoder Representations from Transformers(BERT) scale quadratically with sequence length, making long context modeling expensive. Linear time state space models, such as Mamba, are efficient; however, they show limitations in modeling global interactions and can suffer from padding induced state contamination. We propose MaBERT, a hybrid encoder that interleaves Transformer layers for global dependency modeling with Mamba layers for linear time state updates. This design alternates global contextual integration with fast state accumulation, enabling efficient training and inference on long inputs. To stabilize variable length batching, we introduce paddingsafe masking, which blocks state propagation through padded positions, and mask aware attention pooling, which aggregates information only from valid tokens. On GLUE, MaBERT achieves the best mean score on five of the eight tasks, with strong performance on the CoLA and sentence pair inference tasks. When extending the context from 512 to 4,096 tokens, MaBERT reduces training time and inference latency by 2.36x and 2.43x, respectively, relative to the average of encoder baselines, demonstrating a practical long context efficient encoder.

item.title: ACE-Brain-0: Spatial Intelligence as a Shared Scaffold for Universal Embodiments
item.content:encoded: Universal embodied intelligence demands robust generalization across heterogeneous embodiments, such as autonomous driving, robotics, and unmanned aerial vehicles (UAVs). However, existing embodied brain in training a unified model over diverse embodiments frequently triggers long-tail data, gradient interference, and catastrophic forgetting, making it notoriously difficult to balance universal generalization with domain-specific proficiency. In this report, we introduce ACE-Brain-0, a generalist foundation brain that unifies spatial reasoning, autonomous driving, and embodied manipulation within a single multimodal large language model~(MLLM). Our key insight is that spatial intelligence serves as a universal scaffold across diverse physical embodiments: although vehicles, robots, and UAVs differ drastically in morphology, they share a common need for modeling 3D mental space, making spatial cognition a natural, domain-agnostic foundation for cross-embodiment transfer. Building on this insight, we propose the Scaffold-Specialize-Reconcile~(SSR) paradigm, which first establishes a shared spatial foundation, then cultivates domain-specialized experts, and finally harmonizes them through data-free model merging. Furthermore, we adopt Group Relative Policy Optimization~(GRPO) to strengthen the model's comprehensive capability. Extensive experiments demonstrate that ACE-Brain-0 achieves competitive and even state-of-the-art performance across 24 spatial and embodiment-related benchmarks.

item.title: Infinite dimensional generative sensing
item.content:encoded: Deep generative models have become a standard for modeling priors for inverse problems, going beyond classical sparsity-based methods. However, existing theoretical guarantees are mostly confined to finite-dimensional vector spaces, creating a gap when the physical signals are modeled as functions in Hilbert spaces. This work presents a rigorous framework for generative compressed sensing in Hilbert spaces. We extend the notion of local coherence in an infinite-dimensional setting, to derive optimal, resolution-independent sampling distributions. Thanks to a generalization of the Restricted Isometry Property, we show that stable recovery holds when the number of measurements is proportional to the prior's intrinsic dimension (up to logarithmic factors), independent of the ambient dimension. Finally, numerical experiments on the Darcy flow equation validate our theoretical findings and demonstrate that in severely undersampled regimes, employing lower-resolution generators acts as an implicit regularizer, improving reconstruction stability.

item.title: Stabilized Adaptive Loss and Residual-Based Collocation for Physics-Informed Neural Networks
item.content:encoded: Physics-Informed Neural Networks (PINNs) have been recognized as a mesh-free alternative to solve partial differential equations where physics information is incorporated. However, in dealing with problems characterized by high stiffness or shock-dominated dynamics, traditional PINNs have been found to have limitations, including unbalanced training and inaccuracy in solution, even with small physics residuals. In this research, we seek to address these limitations using the viscous Burgers' equation with low viscosity and the Allen-Cahn equation as test problems. In addressing unbalanced training, we have developed a new adaptive loss balancing scheme using smoothed gradient norms to ensure satisfaction of initial and boundary conditions. Further, to address inaccuracy in the solution, we have developed an adaptive residual-based collocation scheme to improve the accuracy of solutions in the regions with high physics residuals. The proposed new approach significantly improves solution accuracy with consistent satisfaction of physics residuals. For instance, in the case of Burgers' equation, the relative L2 error is reduced by about 44 percent compared to traditional PINNs, while for the Allen-Cahn equation, the relative L2 error is reduced by approximately 70 percent. Additionally, we show the trustworthy solution comparison of the proposed method using a robust finite difference solver.

item.title: 基于SAE的模型迁移性评分方法研究
item.content:encoded:
提出一种新的模型迁移性评分方法，用于预测预训练模型的微调效果。



摘要： 本文研究了预训练大语言模型在下游任务中的迁移性能问题。通过引入基于稀疏自编码器（SAE）的迁移性评分（STS），该方法能够提前预测模型在微调后的表现。实验表明，STS与实际性能变化具有高度相关性，相关系数超过0.7。此外，研究还初步探索了STS在强化学习中的应用潜力，认为其可作为指导后训练策略的可解释工具。

item.title: EIMC：一种高效的多模态协同感知方法
item.content:encoded:
EIMC提升自动驾驶安全，减少通信带宽消耗。



摘要： 本文提出了一种名为EIMC的新型多模态协同感知方法，旨在提升自动驾驶的安全性。与传统方法不同，EIMC采用早期协同范式，在本地融合阶段引入轻量级协同体素，生成紧凑且信息丰富的3D协同先验，从而增强跨模态对齐。随后，通过热图驱动的共识协议识别需要协作的区域，并仅查询低置信度、高差异区域内的Top-K实例向量进行跨注意力融合。最后，通过精炼融合进一步提升关键遮挡目标的检测效果。实验表明，EIMC在OPV2V和DAIR-V2X数据集上取得了73.01%的AP@0.5性能，同时将带宽使用量减少了87.98%。代码已公开。

item.title: 面向科学领域的动态多模型协同框架
item.content:encoded:
提出一种科学领域动态多模型协同框架，提升复杂推理能力。



摘要： 本文针对现有多智能体大语言模型框架在科学和知识密集型领域表现不佳的问题，提出了一种面向科学领域的交互式双层多模型编排框架。该框架通过专用编排模型分析任务，动态构建领域感知的推理流程，并实例化具有定制提示的专家代理；执行模型则按照生成的角色和指令规范完成每一步操作。编排器根据中间反馈迭代更新流程，实现动态重规划、角色重新分配和提示优化，增强科学推理的鲁棒性和专业化。该框架支持异构大语言模型集成，具备灵活的性能与效率权衡能力，在多种推理和科学风格基准测试中均表现出色。

item.title: LLM时代下TeX的局限与Mogan STEM的替代方案
item.content:encoded:
本文分析TeX在LLM时代的不足并介绍Mogan STEM作为更优替代。



摘要： 随着大语言模型（LLMs）在科学写作中的广泛应用，TeX在编译效率、语义生成、错误定位和工具生态方面的缺陷日益凸显。本文深入分析了TeX的这些局限性，并提出Mogan STEM作为一种结构化编辑器的替代方案。Mogan通过高效的数据结构、快速渲染和按需插件加载，在多个方面优于TeX。实验结果表明，Mogan在编译/渲染时间和LLM任务性能上表现更佳。此外，由于Mogan文档格式.tmu具有更低的信息熵，它在微调LLM时更加高效。因此，文章呼吁开展更大规模的基于.tmu格式的LLM训练实验。

item.title: 多物体阴影生成方法研究
item.content:encoded:
提出一种多物体阴影生成方法，提升图像合成效果。



摘要： 本文针对图像合成中多物体阴影生成的问题，提出了一种基于预训练文本到图像扩散模型的方法。该方法通过图像路径和文本路径协同工作，提供细粒度的空间引导并融合物体阴影的边界框信息，利用注意力对齐损失确保阴影区域的准确性。为支持该任务，作者扩展了DESOBAv2数据集，构建了包含多个插入物体的合成场景，并自动生成结合物体类别和阴影位置信息的提示。实验结果表明，该方法在单物体和多物体阴影生成任务中均取得了最佳性能。

item.title: 用于评估腹腔内软组织3D重建的D4D数据集
item.content:encoded:
D4D数据集提供手术条件下软组织3D重建的视频与几何数据。



摘要： D4D数据集包含从六只猪尸体实验中获取的立体内窥镜视频和高精度结构光几何数据，用于评估真实手术环境下腹部软组织的3D重建。数据通过光学跟踪和手动迭代对齐方法进行配准，包含三种序列类型以测试算法在非刚性运动、变形程度和视图外更新方面的鲁棒性。每段视频提供校正后的立体图像、工具掩码、深度信息、点云及相机姿态等数据。该数据集包含超过30万帧和369个点云，适用于非刚性SLAM、4D重建和深度估计方法的开发与评估。

item.title: 面向教育的AI生成视频质量评估基准与方法研究
item.content:encoded:
提出首个教育类AI视频质量评估框架EduVQA，提升AI教学视频质量。



摘要： 本文针对AI生成视频在教育领域的应用潜力不足问题，提出了EduAIGV-1k数据集和评估框架，用于评估AI生成视频在教授基础数学概念方面的质量。该数据集包含1,130个由先进文本到视频模型生成的视频，每个视频配有精细标注，涵盖感知质量和提示对齐两个维度。基于此，作者引入了EduVQA系统，并提出S2D-MoE模块以提升整体质量评估效果。实验表明EduVQA优于现有方法，相关数据和代码将公开。

item.title: 可解释时间序列预测方法IPL的提出与应用
item.content:encoded:
本文提出可解释多项式学习方法，提升时间序列预测的可解释性。



摘要： 时间序列预测在资产性能管理中发挥重要作用，但现有方法缺乏可解释性，影响用户信任和调试效率。本文提出可解释多项式学习（IPL）方法，通过多项式表示显式建模原始特征及其交互，保留时间依赖性并提供特征级可解释性。该方法在模拟数据和比特币价格数据上表现出高精度和良好可解释性，并在实际天线数据中验证了其简单高效的早期预警能力。

item.title: StitchCUDA：面向端到端GPU程序生成的多智能体框架
item.content:encoded:
StitchCUDA提升GPU程序生成效率与性能。



摘要： 本文提出StitchCUDA，一个用于端到端GPU程序生成的多智能体框架，包含规划者、编码器和验证器三个核心组件。该框架通过基于规则的代理强化学习，提升了编码器在任务到代码生成和反馈驱动优化方面的能力，并有效防止了奖励滥用问题。实验表明，StitchCUDA在端到端GPU编程任务中成功率接近100%，比多智能体基线快1.72倍，比强化学习模型基线快2.73倍。

item.title: 基于NEMF框架的物理反演方法构建功能性数字孪生
item.content:encoded:
NEMF框架通过物理反演实现高精度材料参数重建，构建可模拟的数字孪生。



摘要： 本文提出NEMF框架，用于通过非接触式传感技术重建场景中的材料属性，从而构建功能性的数字孪生。传统方法如NeRF虽能生成视觉逼真的3D模型，但缺乏实际物理属性。NEMF通过解耦几何、环境场和材料属性，将原本病态的物理反演问题转化为物理监督的学习任务。该框架利用图像获取高保真几何信息，结合射频信号进行材料参数重建，实现了对场景中材料属性的连续空间分布建模。实验表明，该方法在合成数据集上具有高精度，能够生成可进行高保真物理仿真的数字孪生。

item.title: 基于LLM内部表示的回归预测统计量研究
item.content:encoded:
LLM内部表示可捕捉数值预测的统计特性，提升预测效率。



摘要： 本文研究了大型语言模型（LLMs）在回归任务中的应用，特别是其在连续值输出时的预测分布问题。传统方法依赖自回归解码，计算成本高。本文提出通过训练回归探针，直接从LLM的内部表示中预测统计量（如均值、中位数、分位数），发现LLM嵌入包含关于预测分布的有用信息，包括数值不确定性。该研究为减少采样依赖、提升不确定性感知预测提供了新思路。

item.title: 基于解耦系统的物体抓取框架Unveiler研究
item.content:encoded:
Unveiler通过解耦空间推理与动作执行提升复杂环境下的物体抓取效率。



摘要： 本文提出Unveiler框架，通过将高阶空间推理与低阶动作执行解耦，提升在密集杂乱环境中物体抓取的效率和成功率。核心是轻量级Transformer结构的空间关系编码器（SRE），用于逐步识别关键障碍物，再由旋转不变的动作解码器执行操作。SRE分两阶段训练：模仿学习提供高效初始化，PPO微调使策略超越传统启发式方法。实验表明，在模拟环境中，Unveiler在部分遮挡和完全遮挡场景中分别达到97.6%和90.0%的成功率，并且其空间推理能力可零样本迁移到真实场景，无需重新训练模型组件。

item.title: 分布式随机优化中的梯度延迟框架研究
item.content:encoded:
研究分布式随机优化中梯度延迟问题的解决方法。



摘要： 本文提出了一种适用于梯度延迟情况下的分布式随机优化通用框架。在该框架中，多个本地代理利用自身数据和计算能力协助中心服务器最小化全局目标函数。每个代理可以传输其局部梯度的随机估计值，这些估计可能带有偏差和延迟。尽管已有研究建议采用自适应步长来应对延迟，但本文证明固定递减步长同样有效，并且在非凸和强凸目标函数上能够达到最优SGD性能。

item.title: 基于函数ANOVA的模型可解释性方法研究
item.content:encoded:
提出一种新的函数ANOVA方法用于模型解释。



摘要： 本文提出了一种基于函数ANOVA的模型可解释性方法，解决了依赖特征下模型预测分解的难题。通过结合函数分析与离散傅里叶分析，该方法在不假设分布形式的情况下，实现了对分类输入的闭式分解。该方法计算高效，能够无缝恢复独立情况下的经典结果，并扩展到任意依赖结构。同时，利用SHAP与ANOVA在独立情况下的联系，该框架自然推广了SHAP值至一般分类场景。

item.title: 基于知识库与逻辑推理的辩论分析框架
item.content:encoded:
提出一种透明的辩论分析方法，结合论证挖掘与逻辑推理。



摘要： 本文针对大语言模型在复杂文本推理上的不足，提出一个整合学习型论证挖掘、定量推理和本体查询的框架。该框架从原始辩论文本中提取模糊论证知识库，将论证表示为实体，并通过攻击和支持关系进行连接，同时标注初始模糊强度。随后应用定量论证语义计算最终论证强度，并将其嵌入模糊描述逻辑中，实现高效的查询回答。该方法提供了一种透明、可解释且形式化的方法来分析辩论，弥补了传统统计方法的局限性。

item.title: AI代理行为多样性研究与角色建模
item.content:encoded:
通过角色建模分析AI代理的行为多样性。



摘要： 本文利用Persona Ecosystem Playground（PEP）对Moltbook平台上的41,300条帖子进行分析，通过k-means聚类和检索增强生成技术生成并验证了对话角色。跨角色验证显示，角色与其所属集群的语义相似度高于与其他集群的相似度。随后，在九轮结构化讨论中，模拟消息被显著地归因于其来源角色。研究结果表明，基于角色的生态系统建模可以有效表征AI代理群体中的行为多样性。

item.title: 基于轨迹一致性的Padé近似加速扩散模型采样
item.content:encoded:
提出TC-Padé方法提升扩散模型低步数采样效率。



摘要： 尽管扩散模型在生成质量上表现优异，但其迭代采样过程计算开销大。现有特征缓存技术在高步数下有效，但在低步数（20-30步）时效果受限。传统方法难以处理不同去噪阶段的动力学差异，且多项式外推器易产生误差累积。为此，本文提出轨迹一致的Padé近似（TC-Padé），通过有理函数建模特征演化，更准确捕捉渐近和过渡行为。该方法包含自适应系数调制和步长感知预测策略，显著提升采样效率。实验表明，在FLUX.1-dev和Wan2.1等模型上，TC-Padé实现2.88倍和1.72倍加速，同时保持高质量生成结果。

item.title: 基于大语言模型的船舶轨迹预测框架ShipTraj-R1研究
item.content:encoded:
提出ShipTraj-R1框架，提升船舶轨迹预测精度。



摘要： 本文提出ShipTraj-R1，一个基于大语言模型的船舶轨迹预测框架。通过将轨迹预测任务转化为文本生成问题，并引入动态提示和规则奖励机制，增强模型的推理能力。该框架采用GRPO优化方法，并以Qwen3为模型基础，在两个真实海事数据集上实验显示，其预测误差最小，优于现有深度学习和LLM基线方法。

item.title: 基于大语言模型的科学论文自动优化方法APRES
item.content:encoded:
APRES提升论文质量与影响力，增强科学传播效果。



摘要： 本文提出一种基于大语言模型（LLMs）的新型论文优化方法APRES，旨在通过自动化修订提升科学论文的质量和影响力。该方法利用评估量表预测未来引用次数，并据此对论文进行优化，同时保持核心科学内容不变。实验表明，APRES在平均误差上比现有最佳基线提高了19.6%，且79%的修订论文得到专家评委的青睐。研究强调APRES旨在辅助而非取代人类评审，帮助作者在投稿前更好地测试和改进其研究成果，从而推动科学进步。

item.title: 差分隐私下优化算法的收敛性分析
item.content:encoded:
研究差分隐私噪声对优化算法的影响，对比DP-SGD与DP-SignSGD性能。



摘要： 本文从随机微分方程的角度重新审视差分隐私噪声与优化自适应性的相互作用，首次对私有优化器进行了基于SDE的分析。重点比较了在每样本裁剪下的DP-SGD和DP-SignSGD，在固定超参数下，DP-SGD的隐私-效用权衡为O(1/ε²)，而DP-SignSGD的收敛速度与ε成线性关系，且权衡为O(1/ε)。当使用最优学习率时，两者理论表现相近，但DP-SGD的学习率与ε线性相关，而DP-SignSGD则基本不依赖ε。这表明自适应方法在实际应用中更具优势，因其超参数可跨隐私水平迁移。实验结果验证了理论，并扩展至DP-Adam。

item.title: NOVA：无需配对数据的视频编辑框架
item.content:encoded:
NOVA通过稀疏控制与密集合成实现高效视频编辑。



摘要： 本文提出NOVA，一种无需配对数据的视频编辑框架。该框架包含稀疏分支和密集分支，前者通过用户编辑的关键帧提供语义指导，后者则持续整合原始视频的运动和纹理信息以保持高保真和连贯性。此外，NOVA引入退化模拟训练策略，使模型在人工降噪视频上学习运动重建和时间一致性，从而摆脱对配对数据的依赖。实验表明，NOVA在编辑保真度、运动保留和时间一致性方面优于现有方法。

item.title: 基于LLM的零样本图推理自适应子图提取与去噪框架
item.content:encoded:
提出GraphSSR框架解决零样本图推理中的结构噪声问题。



摘要： 本文针对零样本图任务中因数据稀缺和传统GNN泛化能力不足的问题，提出GraphSSR框架，通过自适应子图提取和去噪提升基于大语言模型的图推理性能。该框架采用“采样-选择-推理”流程动态调整子图结构，减少无关邻接点和边的影响，并结合监督微调和强化学习策略优化推理过程，从而提高预测准确性。

item.title: SUN：跨模型解码执行的高效共享方法
item.content:encoded:
SUN实现跨模型解码共享，提升GPU利用率与系统吞吐。



摘要： 在多模型大语言模型服务中，由于模型特定的资源划分，解码执行效率低下，尤其在负载不均时导致GPU利用率不足。为此，研究提出SUN（Shared Use of Next-token Prediction），首次实现去中心化多LLM服务中的跨模型解码共享。SUN将解码器仅Transformer分解为预填充模块和解码模块，仅微调任务特定的预填充模块，使冻结的解码模块可跨模型共享。该设计支持模型无关的解码路由策略，平衡解码请求以最大化利用率。SUN在多种任务和模型家族中实现了与全微调相当的准确性，同时减少解码工作者数量并保持系统吞吐。实验显示，SUN在传统解耦架构上提升了2.0倍的每GPU吞吐量，且时间每输出标记（TPOT）保持在5%以内。此外，SUN还支持低比特解码，通过量化SUN（QSUN）实现45%的速度提升，同时保持共享解码的优势。

item.title: 多模态大语言模型在文档信息提取中的性能评估
item.content:encoded:
多模态大模型在文档信息提取中表现优异，可替代传统OCR流程。



摘要： 本文对多种多模态大语言模型（MLLMs）在商业文档信息提取任务中的性能进行了大规模基准测试。研究提出了一种自动化的层级错误分析框架，用于系统诊断模型的错误模式。实验结果表明，强大的MLLM在仅使用图像输入的情况下，可以达到与OCR增强方法相当的性能，说明OCR可能不再是必需步骤。此外，研究还发现精心设计的模式、示例和指令能够进一步提升MLLM的表现。该工作为文档信息提取技术的发展提供了实用指导和重要见解。

item.title: 视频生成中的可扩展盲水印框架SIGMark
item.content:encoded:
提出SIGMark实现高效无损视频水印，提升AI安全。



摘要： 本文提出SIGMark，一种适用于视频扩散模型的可扩展、无盲提取的水印框架。与传统方法不同，SIGMark通过生成全局帧级伪随机编码初始噪声，降低存储成本并保持噪声分布和多样性，从而实现无损水印。此外，设计了针对因果3D变分自编码器的段组排序模块，增强对时间扰动的鲁棒性。实验表明，SIGMark在时空扰动下仍能保持高比特准确率，具有良好的可扩展性和实用性。

item.title: 跨模型推测预填充在代理大语言模型中的应用
item.content:encoded:
跨模型推测预填充可有效压缩提示，提升推理效率。



摘要： 本文研究了跨模型推测预填充技术，利用不同模型家族中的轻量级草稿模型对目标模型进行提示压缩。实验表明，尽管模型架构和分词器存在差异，基于注意力的标记重要性估计仍能可靠迁移。该方法在多种任务中保持了90~100%的基准性能，甚至在某些情况下因去噪效果而略微提升准确率，同时显著减少了首次token生成时间。这表明推测预填充主要依赖任务先验和语义结构，具有良好的泛化能力，适用于需要多模型协作的代理系统。

item.content:encoded:
SynthCharge生成可验证的EVRPTW实例，支持动态评估模型性能。



摘要： 本文提出SynthCharge，一个参数化生成器，用于创建多样化的电动汽车路径规划问题实例。该生成器考虑了电池容量和充电站位置，并通过可行性筛选确保实例可解。相比静态基准数据集，SynthCharge支持不同规模和时空配置的实例生成，为学习型路径规划模型提供动态评估平台。实验聚焦于5至100个客户的实例，强调其在神经路由和数据驱动方法中的应用价值。

item.title: Valet：用于评估不完全信息博弈算法的测试平台
item.content:encoded:
Valet是一个用于评估不完全信息博弈算法的多样化测试平台。



摘要： 本文介绍了Valet，一个包含21种传统不完全信息纸牌游戏的综合性测试平台，旨在促进不完全信息博弈算法的研究与比较。这些游戏涵盖多种类型、文化背景和规则机制。为了统一实现，所有游戏规则均以RECYCLE语言编码。通过随机模拟，作者分析了每款游戏的分支因子和持续时间，并提供了蒙特卡洛树搜索玩家对随机对手的基准分数分布，证明了Valet作为基准测试套件的有效性。

item.title: SSD：一种加速自回归解码的并行方法
item.content:encoded:
SSD通过并行化推测与验证提升解码效率。



摘要： 文章提出了一种名为SSD（Speculative Speculative Decoding）的新型解码方法，旨在解决传统自回归解码和推测解码中的序列依赖问题。SSD通过在验证过程中提前预测可能的验证结果，并预先生成推测内容，从而在实际验证结果匹配时直接返回推测结果，大幅减少推理时间。研究识别了SSD面临的三个关键挑战，并提出了相应的解决方案，最终实现了一个优化的算法Saguaro。实验表明，该方法在开源推理引擎中比优化的推测解码基线快2倍，比自回归解码快5倍。

item.title: 混合架构在上下文检索任务中的性能比较研究
item.content:encoded:
混合模型在数据效率和信息密集型检索中表现优于SSMs。



摘要： 本文研究了结合Transformer和状态空间模型（SSM）的混合架构在两种合成上下文检索任务中的表现。第一项任务是n-gram检索，第二项是位置检索。实验表明，混合模型在数据效率和信息密集型任务中优于SSMs，并接近Transformer的表现，但在位置检索上仍不如Transformer。分析发现，SSM模型生成的嵌入具有局部性特征，这解释了其在不同任务中的优劣势。研究为模型选择提供了理论依据。
