item.title: Heterogeneous Agent Collaborative Reinforcement Learning
item.content:encoded: We introduce Heterogeneous Agent Collaborative Reinforcement Learning (HACRL), a new learning paradigm that addresses the inefficiencies of isolated on-policy optimization. HACRL enables collaborative optimization with independent execution: heterogeneous agents share verified rollouts during training to mutually improve, while operating independently at inference time. Unlike LLM-based multi-agent reinforcement learning (MARL), HACRL does not require coordinated deployment, and unlike on-/off-policy distillation, it enables bidirectional mutual learning among heterogeneous agents rather than one-directional teacher-to-student transfer. Building on this paradigm, we propose HACPO, a collaborative RL algorithm that enables principled rollout sharing to maximize sample utilization and cross-agent knowledge transfer. To mitigate capability discrepancies and policy distribution shifts, HACPO introduces four tailored mechanisms with theoretical guarantees on unbiased advantage estimation and optimization correctness. Extensive experiments across diverse heterogeneous model combinations and reasoning benchmarks show that HACPO consistently improves all participating agents, outperforming GSPO by an average of 3.3\% while using only half the rollout cost.

item.title: A Natural Language Agentic Approach to Study Affective Polarization
item.content:encoded: Affective polarization has been central to political and social studies, with growing focus on social media, where partisan divisions are often exacerbated. Real-world studies tend to have limited scope, while simulated studies suffer from insufficient high-quality training data, as manually labeling posts is labor-intensive and prone to subjective biases. The lack of adequate tools to formalize different definitions of affective polarization across studies complicates result comparison and hinders interoperable frameworks. We present a multi-agent model providing a comprehensive approach to studying affective polarization in social media. To operationalize our framework, we develop a platform leveraging large language models (LLMs) to construct virtual communities where agents engage in discussions. We showcase the potential of our platform by (1) analyzing questions related to affective polarization, as explored in social science literature, providing a fresh perspective on this phenomenon, and (2) introducing scenarios that allow observation and measurement of polarization at different levels of granularity and abstraction. Experiments show that our platform is a flexible tool for computational studies of complex social dynamics such as affective polarization. It leverages advanced agent models to simulate rich, context-sensitive interactions and systematically explore research questions traditionally addressed through human-subject studies.

item.title: See and Remember: A Multimodal Agent for Web Traversal
item.content:encoded: Autonomous web navigation requires agents to perceive complex visual environments and maintain long-term context, yet current Large Language Model (LLM) based agents often struggle with spatial disorientation and navigation loops. In this paper, we propose generally applicable V-GEMS(Visual Grounding and Explicit Memory System), a robust multimodal agent architecture designed for precise and resilient web traversal. Our agent integrates visual grounding to resolve ambiguous interactive elements and introduces an explicit memory stack with state tracking. This dual mechanism allows the agent to maintain a structured map of its traversal path, enabling valid backtracking and preventing cyclical failures in deep navigation tasks. We also introduce an updatable dynamic benchmark to rigorously evaluate adaptability. Experiments show V-GEMS significantly dominates the WebWalker baseline, achieving a substantial 28.7% performance gain. Code is available at https://github.com/Vaultttttttttttt/V-GEMS.

item.title: HiLoRA: Hierarchical Low-Rank Adaptation for Personalized Federated Learning
item.content:encoded: Vision Transformers (ViTs) have been widely adopted in vision tasks due to their strong transferability. In Federated Learning (FL), where full fine-tuning is communication heavy, Low-Rank Adaptation (LoRA) provides an efficient and communication-friendly way to adapt ViTs. However, existing LoRA-based federated tuning methods overlook latent client structures in real-world settings, limiting shared representation learning and hindering effective adaptation to unseen clients. To address this, we propose HiLoRA, a hierarchical LoRA framework that places adapters at three levels: root, cluster, and leaf, each designed to capture global, subgroup, and client-specific knowledge, respectively. Through cross-tier orthogonality and cascaded optimization, HiLoRA separates update subspaces and aligns each tier with its residual personalized objective. In particular, we develop a LoRA-Subspace Adaptive Clustering mechanism that infers latent client groups via subspace similarity analysis, thereby facilitating knowledge sharing across structurally aligned clients. Theoretically, we establish a tier-wise generalization analysis that supports HiLoRA's design. Experiments on ViT backbones with CIFAR-100 and DomainNet demonstrate consistent improvements in both personalization and generalization.

item.title: 3D-DRES: Detailed 3D Referring Expression Segmentation
item.content:encoded: Current 3D visual grounding tasks only process sentence level detection or segmentation, which critically fails to leverage the rich compositional contextual reasonings within natural language expressions. To address this challenge, we introduce Detailed 3D Referring Expression Segmentation (3D-DRES), a new task that provides a phrase to 3D instance mapping, aiming at enhancing fine-grained 3D vision language understanding. To support 3D-DRES, we present DetailRefer, a new dataset comprising 54,432 descriptions spanning 11,054 distinct objects. Unlike previous datasets, DetailRefer implements a pioneering phrase-instance annotation paradigm where each referenced noun phrase is explicitly mapped to its corresponding 3D elements. Additionally, we introduce DetailBase, a purposefully streamlined yet effective baseline architecture that supports dual-mode segmentation at both sentence and phrase levels. Our experimental results demonstrate that models trained on DetailRefer not only excel at phrase-level segmentation but also show surprising improvements on traditional 3D-RES benchmarks.

item.title: Why Does RLAIF Work At All?
item.content:encoded: Reinforcement Learning from AI Feedback (RLAIF) enables language models to improve by training on their own preference judgments, yet no theoretical account explains why this self-improvement seemingly works for value learning. We propose the latent value hypothesis, that pretraining on internet-scale data encodes human values as directions in representation space, and constitutional prompts elicit these latent values into preference judgments. We formalize this intuition under a linear model where the constitution acts as a projection operator selecting value-relevant directions. Our analysis yields several results. RLAIF improves alignment when the constitution-activated direction correlates with true values better than the model's default generation direction thus explaining the generation-judgment gap; the ceiling on RLAIF quality is determined by how well representations encode values, which scales with model capacity; and adversarial constitutions exist that can activate anti-social value directions encoded from harmful pretraining data. Our account unifies scattered empirical findings including the refusal direction, low-rank safety subspaces, and RLAIF scaling behavior.

item.title: Gravity Falls: A Comparative Analysis of Domain-Generation Algorithm (DGA) Detection Methods for Mobile Device Spearphishing
item.content:encoded: Mobile devices are frequent targets of eCrime threat actors through SMS spearphishing (smishing) links that leverage Domain Generation Algorithms (DGA) to rotate hostile infrastructure. Despite this, DGA research and evaluation largely emphasize malware C2 and email phishing datasets, leaving limited evidence on how well detectors generalize to smishing-driven domain tactics outside enterprise perimeters. This work addresses that gap by evaluating traditional and machine-learning DGA detectors against Gravity Falls, a new semi-synthetic dataset derived from smishing links delivered between 2022 and 2025. Gravity Falls captures a single threat actor's evolution across four technique clusters, shifting from short randomized strings to dictionary concatenation and themed combo-squatting variants used for credential theft and fee/fine fraud. Two string-analysis approaches (Shannon entropy and Exp0se) and two ML-based detectors (an LSTM classifier and COSSAS DGAD) are assessed using Top-1M domains as benign baselines. Results are strongly tactic-dependent: performance is highest on randomized-string domains but drops on dictionary concatenation and themed combo-squatting, with low recall across multiple tool/cluster pairings. Overall, both traditional heuristics and recent ML detectors are ill-suited for consistently evolving DGA tactics observed in Gravity Falls, motivating more context-aware approaches and providing a reproducible benchmark for future evaluation.

item.title: Semi-Supervised Few-Shot Adaptation of Vision-Language Models
item.content:encoded: Vision-language models (VLMs) pre-trained on large, heterogeneous data sources are becoming increasingly popular, providing rich multi-modal embeddings that enable efficient transfer to new tasks. A particularly relevant application is few-shot adaptation, where only a handful of annotated examples are available to adapt the model through multi-modal linear probes. In medical imaging, specialized VLMs have shown promising performance in zero- and few-shot image classification, which is valuable for mitigating the high cost of expert annotations. However, challenges remain in extremely low-shot regimes: the inherent class imbalances in medical tasks often lead to underrepresented categories, penalizing overall model performance. To address this limitation, we propose leveraging unlabeled data by introducing an efficient semi-supervised solver that propagates text-informed pseudo-labels during few-shot adaptation. The proposed method enables lower-budget annotation pipelines for adapting VLMs, reducing labeling effort by >50% in low-shot regimes.

item.title: Beyond Factual Correctness: Mitigating Preference-Inconsistent Explanations in Explainable Recommendation
item.content:encoded: LLM-based explainable recommenders can produce fluent explanations that are factually correct, yet still justify items using attributes that conflict with a user's historical preferences. Such preference-inconsistent explanations yield logically valid but unconvincing reasoning and are largely missed by standard hallucination or faithfulness metrics. We formalize this failure mode and propose PURE, a preference-aware reasoning framework following a select-then-generate paradigm. Instead of only improving generation, PURE intervenes in evidence selection, it selects a compact set of multi-hop item-centric reasoning paths that are both factually grounded and aligned with user preference structure, guided by user intent, specificity, and diversity to suppress generic, weakly personalized evidence. The selected evidence is then injected into LLM generation via structure-aware prompting that preserves relational constraints. To measure preference inconsistency, we introduce a feature-level, user-centric evaluation metric that reveals misalignment overlooked by factuality-based measures. Experiments on three real-world datasets show that PURE consistently reduces preference-inconsistent explanations and factual hallucinations while maintaining competitive recommendation accuracy, explanation quality, and inference efficiency. These results highlight that trustworthy explanations require not only factual correctness but also justification aligned with user preferences.

item.title: Chain of World: World Model Thinking in Latent Motion
item.content:encoded: Vision-Language-Action (VLA) models are a promising path toward embodied intelligence, yet they often overlook the predictive and temporal-causal structure underlying visual dynamics. World-model VLAs address this by predicting future frames, but waste capacity reconstructing redundant backgrounds. Latent-action VLAs encode frame-to-frame transitions compactly, but lack temporally continuous dynamic modeling and world knowledge. To overcome these limitations, we introduce CoWVLA (Chain-of-World VLA), a new "Chain of World" paradigm that unifies world-model temporal reasoning with a disentangled latent motion representation. First, a pretrained video VAE serves as a latent motion extractor, explicitly factorizing video segments into structure and motion latents. Then, during pre-training, the VLA learns from an instruction and an initial frame to infer a continuous latent motion chain and predict the segment's terminal frame. Finally, during co-fine-tuning, this latent dynamic is aligned with discrete action prediction by jointly modeling sparse keyframes and action sequences in a unified autoregressive decoder. This design preserves the world-model benefits of temporal reasoning and world knowledge while retaining the compactness and interpretability of latent actions, enabling efficient visuomotor learning. Extensive experiments on robotic simulation benchmarks show that CoWVLA outperforms existing world-model and latent-action approaches and achieves moderate computational efficiency, highlighting its potential as a more effective VLA pretraining paradigm. The project website can be found at https://fx-hit.github.io/cowvla-io.

item.title: Safe and Robust Domains of Attraction for Discrete-Time Systems: A Set-Based Characterization and Certifiable Neural Network Estimation
item.content:encoded: Analyzing nonlinear systems with attracting robust invariant sets (RISs) requires estimating their domains of attraction (DOAs). Despite extensive research, accurately characterizing DOAs for general nonlinear systems remains challenging due to both theoretical and computational limitations, particularly in the presence of uncertainties and state constraints. In this paper, we propose a novel framework for the accurate estimation of safe (state-constrained) and robust DOAs for discrete-time nonlinear uncertain systems with continuous dynamics, open safe sets, compact disturbance sets, and uniformly locally $\ell_p$-stable compact RISs. The notion of uniform $\ell_p$ stability is quite general and encompasses, as special cases, uniform exponential and polynomial stability. The DOAs are characterized via newly introduced value functions defined on metric spaces of compact sets. We establish their fundamental mathematical properties and derive the associated Bellman-type (Zubov-type) functional equations. Building on this characterization, we develop a physics-informed neural network (NN) framework to learn the corresponding value functions by embedding the derived Bellman-type equations directly into the training process. To obtain certifiable estimates of the safe robust DOAs from the learned neural approximations, we further introduce a verification procedure that leverages existing formal verification tools. The effectiveness and applicability of the proposed methodology are demonstrated through four numerical examples involving nonlinear uncertain systems subject to state constraints, and its performance is compared with existing methods from the literature.

item.title: Uni-Skill: Building Self-Evolving Skill Repository for Generalizable Robotic Manipulation
item.content:encoded: While skill-centric approaches leverage foundation models to enhance generalization in compositional tasks, they often rely on fixed skill libraries, limiting adaptability to new tasks without manual intervention. To address this, we propose Uni-Skill, a Unified Skill-centric framework that supports skill-aware planning and facilitates automatic skill evolution. Unlike prior methods that restrict planning to predefined skills, Uni-Skill requests for new skill implementations when existing ones are insufficient, ensuring adaptable planning with self-augmented skill library. To support automatic implementation of diverse skills requested by the planning module, we construct SkillFolder, a VerbNet-inspired repository derived from large-scale unstructured robotic videos. SkillFolder introduces a hierarchical skill taxonomy that captures diverse skill descriptions at multiple levels of abstraction. By populating this taxonomy with large-scale, automatically annotated demonstrations, Uni-Skill shifts the paradigm of skill acquisition from inefficient manual annotation to efficient offline structural retrieval. Retrieved examples provide semantic supervision over behavior patterns and fine-grained references for spatial trajectories, enabling few-shot skill inference without deployment-time demonstrations. Comprehensive experiments in both simulation and real-world settings verify the state-of-the-art performance of Uni-Skill over existing VLM-based skill-centric approaches, highlighting its advanced reasoning capabilities and strong zero-shot generalization across a wide range of novel tasks.

item.title: On the Expressive Power of Transformers for Maxout Networks and Continuous Piecewise Linear Functions
item.content:encoded: Transformer networks have achieved remarkable empirical success across a wide range of applications, yet their theoretical expressive power remains insufficiently understood. In this paper, we study the expressive capabilities of Transformer architectures. We first establish an explicit approximation of maxout networks by Transformer networks while preserving comparable model complexity. As a consequence, Transformers inherit the universal approximation capability of ReLU networks under similar complexity constraints. Building on this connection, we develop a framework to analyze the approximation of continuous piecewise linear functions by Transformers and quantitatively characterize their expressivity via the number of linear regions, which grows exponentially with depth. Our analysis establishes a theoretical bridge between approximation theory for standard feedforward neural networks and Transformer architectures. It also yields structural insights into Transformers: self-attention layers implement max-type operations, while feedforward layers realize token-wise affine transformations.

item.title: CoFL: Continuous Flow Fields for Language-Conditioned Navigation
item.content:encoded: Language-conditioned navigation pipelines often rely on brittle modular components or costly action-sequence generation. To address these limitations, we present CoFL, an end-to-end policy that directly maps a bird's-eye view (BEV) observation and a language instruction to a continuous flow field for navigation. Instead of predicting discrete action tokens or sampling action chunks via iterative denoising, CoFL outputs instantaneous velocities that can be queried at arbitrary 2D projected locations. Trajectories are obtained by numerical integration of the predicted field, producing smooth motion that remains reactive under closed-loop execution. To enable large-scale training, we build a dataset of over 500k BEV image-instruction pairs, each procedurally annotated with a flow field and a trajectory derived from BEV semantic maps built on Matterport3D and ScanNet. By training on a mixed distribution, CoFL significantly outperforms modular Vision-Language Model (VLM)-based planners and generative policy baselines on strictly unseen scenes. Finally, we deploy CoFL zero-shot in real-world experiments with overhead BEV observations across multiple layouts, maintaining reliable closed-loop control and a high success rate.

item.title: On Discriminative vs. Generative classifiers: Rethinking MLLMs for Action Understanding
item.content:encoded: Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs) have advanced open-world action understanding and can be adapted as generative classifiers for closed-set settings by autoregressively generating action labels as text. However, this approach is inefficient, and shared subwords across action labels introduce semantic overlap, leading to ambiguity in generation. In contrast, discriminative classifiers learn task-specific representations with clear decision boundaries, enabling efficient one-step classification without autoregressive decoding. We first compare generative and discriminative classifiers with MLLMs for closed-set action understanding, revealing the superior accuracy and efficiency of the latter. To bridge the performance gap, we design strategies that elevate generative classifiers toward performance comparable with discriminative ones. Furthermore, we show that generative modeling can complement discriminative classifiers, leading to better performance while preserving efficiency. To this end, we propose Generation-Assisted Discriminative~(GAD) classifier for closed-set action understanding. GAD operates only during fine-tuning, preserving full compatibility with MLLM pretraining. Extensive experiments on temporal action understanding benchmarks demonstrate that GAD improves both accuracy and efficiency over generative methods, achieving state-of-the-art results on four tasks across five datasets, including an average 2.5% accuracy gain and 3x faster inference on our largest COIN benchmark.

item.title: Credibility Governance: A Social Mechanism for Collective Self-Correction under Weak Truth Signals
item.content:encoded: Online platforms increasingly rely on opinion aggregation to allocate real-world attention and resources, yet common signals such as engagement votes or capital-weighted commitments are easy to amplify and often track visibility rather than reliability. This makes collective judgments brittle under weak truth signals, noisy or delayed feedback, early popularity surges, and strategic manipulation. We propose Credibility Governance (CG), a mechanism that reallocates influence by learning which agents and viewpoints consistently track evolving public evidence. CG maintains dynamic credibility scores for both agents and opinions, updates opinion influence via credibility-weighted endorsements, and updates agent credibility based on the long-run performance of the opinions they support, rewarding early and persistent alignment with emerging evidence while filtering short-lived noise. We evaluate CG in POLIS, a socio-physical simulation environment that models coupled belief dynamics and downstream feedback under uncertainty. Across settings with initial majority misalignment, observation noise and contamination, and misinformation shocks, CG outperforms vote-based, stake-weighted, and no-governance baselines, yielding faster recovery to the true state, reduced lock-in and path dependence, and improved robustness under adversarial pressure. Our implementation and experimental scripts are publicly available at https://github.com/Wanying-He/Credibility_Governance.

item.title: cPNN: Continuous Progressive Neural Networks for Evolving Streaming Time Series
item.content:encoded: Dealing with an unbounded data stream involves overcoming the assumption that data is identically distributed and independent. A data stream can, in fact, exhibit temporal dependencies (i.e., be a time series), and data can change distribution over time (concept drift). The two problems are deeply discussed, and existing solutions address them separately: a joint solution is absent. In addition, learning multiple concepts implies remembering the past (a.k.a. avoiding catastrophic forgetting in Neural Networks' terminology). This work proposes Continuous Progressive Neural Networks (cPNN), a solution that tames concept drifts, handles temporal dependencies, and bypasses catastrophic forgetting. cPNN is a continuous version of Progressive Neural Networks, a methodology for remembering old concepts and transferring past knowledge to fit the new concepts quickly. We base our method on Recurrent Neural Networks and exploit the Stochastic Gradient Descent applied to data streams with temporal dependencies. Results of an ablation study show a quick adaptation of cPNN to new concepts and robustness to drifts.

item.title: QFlowNet: Fast, Diverse, and Efficient Unitary Synthesis with Generative Flow Networks
item.content:encoded: Unitary Synthesis, the decomposition of a unitary matrix into a sequence of quantum gates, is a fundamental challenge in quantum compilation. Prevailing reinforcement learning(RL) approaches are often hampered by sparse reward signals, which necessitate complex reward shaping or long training times, and typically converge to a single policy, lacking solution diversity. In this work, we propose QFlowNet, a novel framework that learns efficiently from sparse signals by pairing a Generative Flow Network (GFlowNet) with Transformers. Our approach addresses two key challenges. First, the GFlowNet framework is fundamentally designed to learn a diverse policy that samples solutions proportional to their reward, overcoming the single-solution limitation of RL while offering faster inference than other generative models like diffusion. Second, the Transformers act as a powerful encoder, capturing the non-local structure of unitary matrices and compressing a high-dimensional state into a dense latent representation for the policy network. Our agent achieves an overall success rate of 99.7% on a 3-qubit benchmark(lengths 1-12) and discovers a diverse set of compact circuits, establishing QFlowNet as an efficient and diverse paradigm for unitary synthesis.

item.title: Mind the Way You Select Negative Texts: Pursuing the Distance Consistency in OOD Detection with VLMs
item.content:encoded: Out-of-distribution (OOD) detection seeks to identify samples from unknown classes, a critical capability for deploying machine learning models in open-world scenarios. Recent research has demonstrated that Vision-Language Models (VLMs) can effectively leverage their multi-modal representations for OOD detection. However, current methods often incorporate intra-modal distance during OOD detection, such as comparing negative texts with ID labels or comparing test images with image proxies. This design paradigm creates an inherent inconsistency against the inter-modal distance that CLIP-like VLMs are optimized for, potentially leading to suboptimal performance. To address this limitation, we propose InterNeg, a simple yet effective framework that systematically utilizes consistent inter-modal distance enhancement from textual and visual perspectives. From the textual perspective, we devise an inter-modal criterion for selecting negative texts. From the visual perspective, we dynamically identify high-confidence OOD images and invert them into the textual space, generating extra negative text embeddings guided by inter-modal distance. Extensive experiments across multiple benchmarks demonstrate the superiority of our approach. Notably, our InterNeg achieves state-of-the-art performance compared to existing works, with a 3.47\% reduction in FPR95 on the large-scale ImageNet benchmark and a 5.50\% improvement in AUROC on the challenging Near-OOD benchmark.

item.title: Rethinking Time Series Domain Generalization via Structure-Stratified Calibration
item.content:encoded: For time series arising from latent dynamical systems, existing cross-domain generalization methods commonly assume that samples are comparably meaningful within a shared representation space. In real-world settings, however, different datasets often originate from structurally heterogeneous families of dynamical systems, leading to fundamentally distinct feature distributions. Under such circumstances, performing global alignment while neglecting structural differences is highly prone to establishing spurious correspondences and inducing negative transfer. From the new perspective of cross-domain structural correspondence failure, we revisit this problem and propose a structurally stratified calibration framework. This approach explicitly distinguishes structurally consistent samples and performs amplitude calibration exclusively within structurally compatible sample clusters, thereby effectively alleviating generalization failures caused by structural incompatibility. Notably, the proposed framework achieves substantial performance improvements through a concise and computationally efficient calibration strategy. Evaluations on 19 public datasets (100.3k samples) demonstrate that SSCF significantly outperforms strong baselines under the zero-shot setting. These results confirm that establishing structural consistency prior to alignment constitutes a more reliable and effective pathway for improving cross-domain generalization of time series governed by latent dynamical systems.

item.title: 基于背景的隐私保护框架PPEDCRF在自动驾驶视频中的应用
item.content:encoded:
PPEDCRF有效保护驾驶视频中的位置隐私，同时保持检测性能。



摘要： 本文研究了通过背景视觉线索进行位置隐私泄露的问题，并提出了一种名为PPEDCRF的隐私保护框架。该框架通过动态条件随机场、归一化控制惩罚和实用噪声注入模块，在不影响目标检测与分割性能的前提下，对敏感背景区域进行精确扰动，从而有效降低位置检索攻击的成功率。实验表明，PPEDCRF在多个公开数据集上表现优于传统方法。

item.title: LLM代理自动微调的探索与实验
item.content:encoded:
LLM代理可有效实现自动化微调，提升模型性能。



摘要： 文章探讨了使用基于大语言模型的代理来实现端到端的模型微调，以解决传统方法中耗时且昂贵的问题。研究提出FT-Dojo环境和FT-Agent系统，通过评估驱动的反馈机制，使代理能够自主优化数据处理、训练流程和策略。实验表明，专门设计的微调代理在多个任务中表现优于通用方法，尤其在10项任务中取得最佳成绩。研究还揭示了代理在因果推理方面的局限性，展示了自动化微调的潜力与挑战。

item.title: ASTRA-bench：评估下一代AI在复杂上下文与多步骤推理中的表现
item.content:encoded:
ASTRA-bench评估AI在复杂个人上下文和多步骤任务中的表现。



摘要： 文章介绍了ASTRA-bench，这是一个新的基准测试，用于评估AI在处理大量个人数据、多样化工具和多步骤推理任务中的能力。该基准结合了时间演化的个人上下文、交互式工具箱和复杂用户意图，生成了2413个场景。实验表明，当前最先进的模型在高复杂度环境下表现不佳，尤其是在生成论点方面存在瓶颈。研究揭示了现有AI助手在处理混乱个人上下文和制定可靠多步骤计划方面的局限性，并提供了完整的执行环境和评估脚本以支持开发更智能的AI助手。

item.title: AI内容审核的挑战：共存违规与动态规则下的判断困境
item.content:encoded:
AI在处理多违规和动态规则时面临判断能力下降的问题。



摘要： 文章探讨了AI在在线内容审核中的核心挑战，特别是在处理共存违规（如同时违反偏见和人身攻击政策）以及动态规则（随上下文变化的平台指南）时的表现。尽管大语言模型在遵循固定规则方面表现良好，但在面对不稳定的政策或情境依赖的判断时，其能力明显下降，导致审核结果不一致，可能错误限制合法表达或放任有害内容。这引发了对现有静态基准测试是否能有效评估AI在现实场景中判断能力的质疑。

item.title: Mix-GRM：通过结构化推理机制提升生成奖励模型性能
item.content:encoded:
Mix-GRM通过结构化B-CoT和D-CoT提升奖励模型性能。



摘要： 本文提出Mix-GRM框架，通过模块化合成流程将原始推理转化为结构化的B-CoT（多维原则覆盖）和D-CoT（实质性判断严谨性），并结合SFT和RLVR优化推理机制。实验表明，Mix-GRM在五个基准测试中达到新最佳，平均优于领先开源模型8.2%。研究发现B-CoT适用于主观偏好任务，D-CoT更擅长客观正确性任务，且RLVR能引导模型根据任务需求自动调整推理风格。相关数据和代码已开源。

item.title: mtLoRA：解决多任务LoRA扩展性能下降的高效方法
item.content:encoded:
mtLoRA提升多任务LoRA性能，减少参数和训练时间。



摘要： 文章指出，当将多任务低秩适配（LoRA）扩展到大量任务时，会出现严重的性能下降，如在DOTA数据集上从88.2%准确率降至2.0%。这种失败源于参数和表示的不一致。现有解决方案如正则化和动态路由在大规模应用中失效，因为它们面临一个根本性权衡：增强正则化以减少任务间冲突会抑制有效路由所需的特征区分度。本文分析了这一权衡的两个根源：一是统一正则化破坏了任务间知识共享，二是组件级LoRA加剧了梯度冲突。基于这些发现，作者提出mtLoRA，包含三个创新设计：谱感知正则化、块级适配和细粒度路由。在四个大规模视觉和NLP基准测试中，mtLoRA表现出色，平均优于现有技术2.3%，同时使用更少参数和训练时间。

item.title: FLANS at SemEval-2026 Task 7: RAG with Open-Sourced Smaller LLMs for Everyday Knowledge Across Diverse Languages and Cultures
item.content:encoded: This system paper describes our participation in the SemEval-2025 Task-7 ``Everyday Knowledge Across Diverse Languages and Cultures''. We attended two subtasks, i.e., Track 1: Short Answer Questions (SAQ), and Track 2: Multiple-Choice Questions (MCQ). The methods we used are retrieval augmented generation (RAGs) with open-sourced smaller LLMs (OS-sLLMs). To better adapt to this shared task, we created our own culturally aware knowledge base (CulKBs) by extracting Wikipedia content using keyword lists we prepared. We extracted both culturally-aware wiki-text and country-specific wiki-summary. In addition to the local CulKBs, we also have one system integrating live online search output via DuckDuckGo. Towards better privacy and sustainability, we aimed to deploy smaller LLMs (sLLMs) that are open-sourced on the Ollama platform. We share the prompts we developed using refinement techniques and report the learning curve of such prompts. The tested languages are English, Spanish, and Chinese for both tracks. Our resources and codes are shared via https://github.com/aaronlifenghan/FLANS-2026

item.title: 基于多粒度序列建模的微视频推荐方法 MealRec
item.content:encoded:
提出 MealRec 解决微视频推荐中的噪声与模态冲突问题。



摘要： 本文针对微视频推荐中因多模态内容噪声和不可靠隐式反馈导致的用户偏好预测困难，提出一种基于多粒度序列建模的推荐方法 MealRec。该方法通过引入时序引导的内容扩散（TCD）和无噪声偏好去噪（NPD），在视频内部和跨视频层面捕捉时间相关性，提升视频表示质量和用户兴趣建模的准确性。实验表明，MealRec在多个平台的数据集上表现出色，具有良好的通用性和鲁棒性。

item.title: 基于图神经网络的对称性一致力场模型用于大尺度晶格系统模拟
item.content:encoded:
图神经网络实现高效、对称性一致的晶格动力学模拟。



摘要： 本文提出一种基于图神经网络（GNN）的力场框架，用于模拟晶格系统的绝热动力学。与传统方法相比，GNN通过局部信息传递和权重共享直接实现晶格对称性，简化了模型设计。该框架在半经典Holstein模型中进行了验证，训练数据来自精确对角化结果，展现出高力精度、线性扩展性和对大规模晶格的良好迁移能力。利用其可扩展性，研究者进行了大尺度朗之万模拟，揭示了电荷密度波有序化过程中的动态标度和异常缓慢的亚Allen-Cahn粗化现象。结果表明，GNN是处理关联晶格系统大规模动力学模拟的一种优雅且高效的架构。

item.title: 流式设置下广义线性预测的动量加速算法
item.content:encoded:
提出首个结合动量的流式优化算法，实现双动量加速。



摘要： 本文研究在流式设置下的广义线性预测问题，其中每次迭代仅使用一个新数据点进行梯度更新。尽管动量在确定性优化中已被广泛使用，但其在单次非二次随机优化中的加速效果仍是未解难题。本文提出了首个成功通过新颖的数据依赖邻近方法引入动量的算法，实现了双动量加速。所推导的过量风险界限分解为三部分：改进的优化误差、最小最大最优统计误差和更高阶的模型不匹配误差。证明通过精细的内更新平稳分析处理模型不匹配，并通过两阶段外循环分析定位统计误差。最终解决了Jain等人（2018a）提出的开放问题，证明动量加速在流式设置下的广义线性预测中比方差减少更有效。

item.title: 面向Society 5.0的智能家庭模拟器代理框架研究
item.content:encoded:
提出S5-HES Agent，实现智能家庭模拟的自动化与多领域适配。



摘要： 本文针对Society 5.0愿景下智能家庭技术发展需求，提出一种基于AI的智能家庭模拟器代理框架（S5-HES Agent）。该框架通过可互换的大语言模型实现自然语言驱动的端到端模拟配置，无需编程技能。采用检索增强生成（RAG）管道，结合语义、关键词和混合搜索方式获取智能家庭知识。实验表明，该框架在设备行为模拟、威胁场景匹配和可扩展性方面表现优异，为安全、能源、健康等多领域研究提供了稳定支持。

item.title: LinkVLA：提升自动驾驶中语言与动作对齐的新型架构
item.content:encoded:
LinkVLA提升自动驾驶中语言与动作对齐和效率。



摘要： 本文提出LinkVLA，一种用于增强自动驾驶中语言与动作对齐和生成效率的新架构。通过将语言和动作标记统一到共享离散代码本中，并引入辅助动作理解目标以建立双向语言-动作映射，从而提高跨模态一致性。同时，采用两步粗到细的生成方法（C2F）替代传统逐步生成，显著提升推理速度。实验表明，该方法在闭环驾驶基准测试中提升了指令遵循准确率和驾驶性能，同时降低了延迟。

item.title: 差分隐私下基于似然的统计推断方法
item.content:encoded:
本文提出一种差分隐私下基于充分统计量的统计推断方法。



摘要： 本文研究了差分隐私数据发布系统中统计推断的问题，提出了一种在指数族模型下释放差分隐私充分统计量的方法，并通过噪声校准的似然推断进行后续分析。作者提出了近似差分隐私释放截断充分统计量的通用方法，证明了插件式差分隐私最大似然估计的渐近正态性及方差膨胀，并引入了一种噪声感知的似然修正方法，支持基于自助法的置信区间。此外，还给出了一个匹配的最小最大下界，表明隐私失真率是不可避免的。该理论为差分隐私合成数据的生成提供了明确的设计规则和实用流程，已在三个指数族模型和真实人口普查数据上得到验证。

item.title: 递归模型突破语言模型的上下文限制
item.content:encoded:
递归模型提升长距离推理能力，优于传统模型。



摘要： 文章探讨了现代语言模型在有限上下文下的推理局限，并提出递归模型作为解决方案。该模型通过自我调用解决子任务，实现更高效的上下文管理。理论证明，任何可计算问题均可被递归分解，每个子任务所需的上下文远小于传统自回归模型。研究还扩展框架至现代代理系统，证明递归模型在更广泛场景中具有最优性能。实验表明，使用递归方法训练的3B模型在布尔可满足性任务中表现显著优于前沿大模型。

item.title: De-paradox Tree：揭示观察数据中悖论性关联的可解释算法
item.content:encoded:
De-paradox Tree识别观察数据中的悖论性关联并提升决策可靠性。



摘要： 文章介绍了一种名为 De-paradox Tree 的可解释算法，用于检测和解释观察性数据中由于混杂因素和子群异质性导致的悖论性关联。该算法通过递归划分、新型分割标准和平衡方法，调整混杂因素并同质化效应，从而提高因果推断的准确性。与现有方法相比，De-paradox Tree 构建更简单、更具解释性的树结构，能够识别嵌套的相反效应，并在因果可接受变量存在时提供稳健的因果效应估计。该方法弥补了传统因果推断和机器学习的不足，为非专家用户提供更可靠的数据驱动决策支持。

item.title: 基于随机神经网络的表面偏微分方程求解方法
item.content:encoded:
提出一种随机神经网络方法高效求解静态与动态表面PDE。



摘要： 本文提出一种基于随机神经网络（RaNN）的方法用于求解静态和动态表面上的偏微分方程。该方法通过随机生成并固定隐藏层参数，仅需通过最小二乘法求解输出层系数，从而避免复杂的训练过程。针对静态表面，提出了参数化、隐式水平集和点云几何的公式，并对参数化方法进行了理论分析。对于保持拓扑结构的动态表面，通过流映射表示表面演化，并在时空配点集上求解PDE，无需重新网格化。实验结果表明该方法具有广泛适用性和良好的精度与效率。

item.title: Linux内核中机器学习基础设施的设计与实现
item.content:encoded:
文章提出在Linux内核中引入机器学习架构的可行性方案。



摘要： 文章探讨了在Linux内核中引入机器学习（ML）的可能性，指出由于内核空间不支持浮点运算单元（FPU），直接应用ML模型可能导致性能下降。为此，作者提出了一个适用于Linux内核的ML基础设施架构，并通过一个概念验证（PoC）项目展示了该方案的可行性。该方案设计了内核空间ML模型代理与用户空间线程之间的交互接口，旨在提升内核配置效率并实现自适应优化。

item.title: 多归因学习基准与方法研究
item.content:encoded:
提出首个多归因CVR数据集与开源框架，推动MAL发展。



摘要： 本文介绍了多归因学习（MAL）在转化率预测中的应用，并指出现有数据集的局限性。为解决这一问题，作者构建了首个包含多归因机制标签的公开CVR数据集MAC，并开发了PyMAL开源库。实验揭示了MAL在不同归因设置下的性能提升、辅助目标选择的重要性以及架构设计原则。基于这些发现，提出了Mixture of Asymmetric Experts（MoAE）方法，在MAC数据集上表现优于现有最佳方法。研究旨在促进MAL领域的进一步探索。

item.title: 基于生成流网络的射线追踪路径采样框架
item.content:encoded:
机器学习提升射线追踪效率与准确性。



摘要： 本文提出一种基于生成流网络的机器学习框架，用于替代传统的穷举路径搜索方法，以解决射线追踪在大规模或实时应用中的计算复杂度瓶颈。该框架通过智能采样提高路径搜索效率，并引入经验回放缓冲、统一探索策略和物理掩码机制，有效应对路径稀疏奖励问题。实验表明，该模型在GPU上速度提升达10倍，在CPU上提升达1000倍，同时保持高覆盖准确率，成功发现复杂传播路径。代码和测试已开源。

item.title: BiCAM：一种用于视觉Transformer解释的双向类激活映射方法
item.content:encoded:
BiCAM通过双向激活映射提升ViT模型的可解释性。



摘要： 本文提出BiCAM，一种用于视觉Transformer（ViTs）的双向类激活映射方法，能够同时捕捉支持性和抑制性特征对模型预测的影响。与以往仅关注正向信号的CAM方法不同，BiCAM保留了负向贡献，生成更全面和对比性的解释。此外，BiCAM引入了正负比（PNR）指标，用于在不重新训练的情况下检测对抗样本。实验表明，BiCAM在ImageNet、VOC和COCO数据集上提升了定位精度和解释可靠性，且计算效率高，适用于多种ViT架构。

item.title: 深度学习回归中的不确定性量化方法改进
item.content:encoded:
提出一种高效方法提升深度回归模型的不确定性量化效果。



摘要： 本文研究了深度学习回归中的不确定性量化问题，特别是在异方差回归场景下，传统方法在均值预测与不确定性估计之间存在优化困难、表征崩溃和方差过拟合等问题。作者提出了一种后处理方法，在预训练网络的中间层上对方差模型进行拟合，从而有效解决上述挑战。实验表明，该方法在多个分子图数据集上实现了与现有方法相当或更优的不确定性量化效果，同时保持均值预测精度，并且推理成本较低。

item.title: 提升医学视觉语言模型临床可靠性的评估方法研究
item.content:encoded:
本文探讨医学视觉语言模型的评估盲点，提出新的临床特异性衡量方法。



摘要： 本文指出当前医学视觉语言模型评估中存在一个关键盲点：虽然模型在文本相似度上表现良好，但可能因模板坍缩而生成重复、缺乏临床术语的报告，导致评估指标失真。作者提出一种基于词汇层面的评估框架——临床关联位移（CAD），用于检测生成报告中不同人群的词义关联变化，并引入加权关联擦除（WAE）来衡量临床信号损失。研究发现，确定性解码会导致语义消失，而随机采样虽能提高多样性，却可能引入新偏见，因此需要重新定义“最佳”报告的标准。

item.title: 基于选择性校准的鲁棒不确定性集估计方法
item.content:encoded:
研究如何在未知因果结构下提升不确定性集的准确性。



摘要： 本文探讨了在未知因果结构的情况下，如何通过选择性校准预测来提高不确定性集的精度。提出了一个对污染数据具有鲁棒性的覆盖定理，量化了错误分类“不受影响”校准样本对覆盖率的影响，并引入了一种任务驱动的部分因果学习方法，仅估计必要的后代指示变量。同时，还提出了基于扰动交集模式的后代发现算法和近似干预距离估计方法。实验表明，在控制污染比例为0.30时，修正后的方法仍能保持至少0.95的覆盖率，而未修正的方法覆盖率下降至0.867。该方法在合成线性结构方程模型和真实基因组筛选数据中均表现出良好的适用性。

item.title: SciDER：端到端自动化科学发现系统
item.content:encoded:
SciDER实现科学发现全流程自动化，提升研究效率。



摘要： 本文介绍了SciDER，一个以数据为中心的端到端科学发现系统。与传统框架不同，SciDER通过专门代理协作解析和分析原始实验数据，生成基于数据特征的假设和实验设计，并编写和执行相应代码。在三个基准测试中，SciDER表现出色，优于通用代理和最先进的模型，其自进化记忆和批评反馈循环是关键优势。SciDER以模块化Python包形式发布，提供易于使用的PyPI包和轻量级网页界面，旨在加速自主数据驱动的研究，使所有研究人员和开发者都能受益。

item.title: 基于显式对应关系的增强现实肝脏手术配准方法
item.content:encoded:
提出Land-Reg框架提升腹腔镜手术中2D-3D图像配准精度。



摘要： 本文提出一种基于显式对应关系的增强现实肝脏手术配准方法Land-Reg，通过学习潜在空间中的2D-3D关键点对应关系，提高术中解剖导航的准确性和稳定性。该方法包含跨模态特征对齐模块、不确定性增强的匹配检测器以及形状约束监督策略，有效解决传统方法在临床场景中对齐不稳定的问题。实验表明，该方法在刚性姿态估计和非刚性变形方面均优于现有技术，代码已开源。

item.title: 多模态个性化记忆问答基准ATM-Bench的提出与评估
item.content:encoded:
提出首个多模态个性化记忆问答基准ATM-Bench，提升AI助手记忆与推理能力。



摘要： 本文介绍了ATM-Bench，这是首个针对多模态、多源个性化参考记忆问答的基准测试。该基准包含四年的隐私保护个人记忆数据和人工标注的问答对，涵盖需要解决个人引用、多证据推理和处理冲突证据的问题。研究提出Schema-Guided Memory（SGM）结构化表示方法，并在五个最先进的记忆系统上进行实验，发现现有系统在ATM-Bench-Hard集上的表现较差，而SGM相比传统描述性记忆方法有显著提升。代码已开源。

item.title: 多领域统一图像修复方法DATPRL-IR的提出
item.content:encoded:
提出DATPRL-IR实现多领域图像修复，性能优越。



摘要： 本文提出一种多领域统一图像修复方法DATPRL-IR，通过构建任务提示池和领域提示池，结合提示组合机制，生成具有领域感知的任务提示表示。该方法能够自适应地整合任务相关知识和领域先验信息，提升图像修复效果。实验表明，DATPRL-IR在多个领域中均优于现有最优方法，展现出强大的泛化能力。

item.title: LTD方法提升大语言模型推理效率
item.content:encoded:
LTD通过强化学习优化生成与验证流程，显著提升大语言模型推理速度。



摘要： 本文提出LTD（Learning to Draft）方法，通过强化学习优化大语言模型的推理过程。该方法在生成候选词和验证阶段之间动态协调，直接优化每个循环的吞吐量，而非依赖静态时间分配或代理指标。实验表明，LTD在多个大语言模型和任务中实现了2.24倍至4.32倍的速度提升，优于现有最佳方法Eagle3达36.4%。

item.title: AnnoABSA：首个支持全谱Aspect-Based Sentiment Analysis的在线标注工具
item.content:encoded:
AnnoABSA是首个支持ABSA任务的在线标注工具，提供LLM辅助生成。



摘要： AnnoABSA是一款基于网页的标注工具，专为Aspect-Based Sentiment Analysis（ABSA）设计，支持从细粒度到粗粒度的全部任务。该工具高度可定制，允许用户灵活配置情感元素和任务需求。除了手动标注外，AnnoABSA还提供基于大语言模型的检索增强生成（RAG）建议，通过上下文感知的方式在人机协作中提供辅助。系统会根据已标注的相似示例，动态添加少量示例以提升预测准确性。AnnoABSA采用MIT许可证开源，便于研究与实际应用扩展。

item.title: 基于加速度计的CNC位置重建方法研究
item.content:encoded:
机器学习模型可有效从加速度计数据中重建CNC轴和刀具位置。



摘要： 本文研究了利用序列到序列的机器学习模型，从加速度计数据中重建CNC机床轴和刀具位置的方法。传统信号处理方法在宽带加速度计信号中难以实现位置重建，而本文提出的基于LSTM的模型能够克服传感器和工艺中的非理想因素，显著降低重建误差。实验结果显示，对于低复杂度运动轨迹，重建误差减少高达98%，对于复杂加工序列，误差减少达85%。该方法还能保留刀具轨迹和工件运动的关键几何特征。这是首个展示从工业条件监测加速度计数据中进行学习式CNC位置重建的研究。

item.title: BAWSeg: A UAV Multispectral Benchmark for Barley Weed Segmentation
item.content:encoded: Accurate weed mapping in cereal fields requires pixel-level segmentation from UAV imagery that remains reliable across fields, seasons, and illumination. Existing multispectral pipelines often depend on thresholded vegetation indices, which are brittle under radiometric drift and mixed crop--weed pixels, or on single-stream CNN and Transformer backbones that ingest stacked bands and indices, where radiance cues and normalized index cues interfere and reduce sensitivity to small weed clusters embedded in crop canopies. We propose VISA (Vegetation-Index and Spectral Attention), a two-stream segmentation network that decouples these cues and fuses them at native resolution. The radiance stream learns from calibrated five-band reflectance using residual spectral-spatial attention to preserve fine textures and row boundaries that are attenuated by ratio indices. The index stream operates on vegetation-index maps with windowed self-attention to model local structure efficiently, state-space layers to propagate field-scale context without quadratic attention cost, and Slot Attention to form stable region descriptors that improve discrimination of sparse weeds under canopy mixing. To support supervised training and deployment-oriented evaluation, we introduce BAWSeg, a four-year UAV multispectral dataset collected over commercial barley paddocks in Western Australia, providing radiometrically calibrated blue, green, red, red edge, and near-infrared orthomosaics, derived vegetation indices, and dense crop, weed, and other labels with leakage-free block splits. On BAWSeg, VISA achieves 75.6% mIoU and 63.5% weed IoU with 22.8M parameters, outperforming a multispectral SegFormer-B1 baseline by 1.2 mIoU and 1.9 weed IoU. Under cross-plot and cross-year protocols, VISA maintains 71.2% and 69.2% mIoU, respectively. The BAWSeg data, VISA code, and trained models will be released upon publication.

item.title: Agentic Code Reasoning
item.content:encoded: Can LLM agents explore codebases and reason about code semantics without executing the code? We study this capability, which we call agentic code reasoning, and introduce semi-formal reasoning: a structured prompting methodology that requires agents to construct explicit premises, trace execution paths, and derive formal conclusions. Unlike unstructured chain-of-thought, semi-formal reasoning acts as a certificate: the agent cannot skip cases or make unsupported claims. We evaluate across three tasks (patch equivalence verification, fault localization, and code question answering) and show that semi-formal reasoning consistently improves accuracy on all of them. For patch equivalence, accuracy improves from 78% to 88% on curated examples and reaches 93% on real-world agent-generated patches, approaching the reliability needed for execution-free RL reward signals. For code question answering on RubberDuckBench Mohammad et al. (2026), semi-formal reasoning achieves 87% accuracy. For fault localization on Defects4J Just et al. (2014), semi-formal reasoning improves Top-5 accuracy by 5 percentage points over standard reasoning. These results demonstrate that structured agentic reasoning enables meaningful semantic code analysis without execution, opening practical applications in RL training pipelines, code review, and static program analysis.

item.title: KDFlow: A User-Friendly and Efficient Knowledge Distillation Framework for Large Language Models
item.content:encoded: Knowledge distillation (KD) is an essential technique to compress large language models (LLMs) into smaller ones. However, despite the distinct roles of the student model and the teacher model in KD, most existing frameworks still use a homogeneous training backend (e.g., FSDP and DeepSpeed) for both models, leading to suboptimal training efficiency. In this paper, we present a novel framework for LLM distillation, termed \textbf{KDFlow}, which features a decoupled architecture and employs SGLang for teacher inference. By bridging the training efficiency of FSDP2 and the inference efficiency of SGLang, KDFlow achieves full utilization of both advantages in a unified system. Moreover, instead of transferring full logits across different processes, our framework only transmits the teacher's hidden states using zero-copy data transfer and recomputes the logits on the student side, effectively balancing the communication cost and KD performance. Furthermore, our framework supports both off-policy and on-policy distillation and incorporates KD algorithms for cross-tokenizer KD through highly extensible and user-friendly APIs. Experiments show that KDFlow can achieve \textbf{1.44$\times$ to 6.36$\times$} speedup compared to current KD frameworks, enabling researchers to rapidly prototype and scale LLM distillation with minimal engineering overhead. Code is available at: https://github.com/songmzhang/KDFlow

item.title: RA-Det: Towards Universal Detection of AI-Generated Images via Robustness Asymmetry
item.content:encoded: Recent image generators produce photo-realistic content that undermines the reliability of downstream recognition systems. As visual appearance cues become less pronounced, appearance-driven detectors that rely on forensic cues or high-level representations lose stability. This motivates a shift from appearance to behavior, focusing on how images respond to controlled perturbations rather than how they look. In this work, we identify a simple and universal behavioral signal. Natural images preserve stable semantic representations under small, structured perturbations, whereas generated images exhibit markedly larger feature drift. We refer to this phenomenon as robustness asymmetry and provide a theoretical analysis that establishes a lower bound connecting this asymmetry to memorization tendencies in generative models, explaining its prevalence across architectures. Building on this insight, we introduce Robustness Asymmetry Detection (RA-Det), a behavior-driven detection framework that converts robustness asymmetry into a reliable decision signal. Evaluated across 14 diverse generative models and against more than 10 strong detectors, RA-Det achieves superior performance, improving the average performance by 7.81 percent. The method is data- and model-agnostic, requires no generator fingerprints, and transfers across unseen generators. Together, these results indicate that robustness asymmetry is a stable, general cue for synthetic-image detection and that carefully designed probing can turn this cue into a practical, universal detector. The source code is publicly available at Github.

item.title: TiledAttention: a CUDA Tile SDPA Kernel for PyTorch
item.content:encoded: TiledAttention is a scaled dot-product attention (SDPA) forward operator for SDPA research on NVIDIA GPUs. Implemented in cuTile Python (TileIR) and exposed as a PyTorch-callable function, it is easier to modify than low-level CUDA templates while retaining realistic behavior via online softmax and tiled $K,V$ streaming. The approach is both performant and directly editable at the schedule level from Python (tile shapes, staging, shared-memory layout), enabling rapid, reproducible kernel research without template-heavy CUDA/CUTLASS rewrites. We benchmark TiledAttention on an NVIDIA DGX GB10 node with a reproducible harness and compare against PyTorch SDPA (auto-dispatch) and explicit unfused baselines across sequence length, head dimension, and precision (FP16/BF16). While production fused baselines remain stronger overall, TiledAttention delivers large speedups over standard eager attention paths and is available for direct use within PyTorch workflows, providing a practical balance between performance and customizability.

item.title: SkeleGuide: Explicit Skeleton Reasoning for Context-Aware Human-in-Place Image Synthesis
item.content:encoded: Generating realistic and structurally plausible human images into existing scenes remains a significant challenge for current generative models, which often produce artifacts like distorted limbs and unnatural poses. We attribute this systemic failure to an inability to perform explicit reasoning over human skeletal structure. To address this, we introduce SkeleGuide, a novel framework built upon explicit skeletal reasoning. Through joint training of its reasoning and rendering stages, SkeleGuide learns to produce an internal pose that acts as a strong structural prior, guiding the synthesis towards high structural integrity. For fine-grained user control, we introduce PoseInverter, a module that decodes this internal latent pose into an explicit and editable format. Extensive experiments demonstrate that SkeleGuide significantly outperforms both specialized and general-purpose models in generating high-fidelity, contextually-aware human images. Our work provides compelling evidence that explicitly modeling skeletal structure is a fundamental step towards robust and plausible human image synthesis.

item.title: Causal Effects with Unobserved Unit Types in Interacting Human-AI Systems
item.content:encoded: We study experiments on interacting populations of humans and AI agents, where both unit types and the interaction network remain unobserved. Although causal effects propagate throughout the system, the goal is to estimate effects on humans. Examples include online platforms where human users interact alongside AI-driven accounts. We assume a human-AI prior that gives each unit a probability of being human. While humans cannot be distinguished at the unit level, the prior allows us to compute the average human composition within large subpopulations. We then model outcome dynamics through a causal message passing (CMP) framework and analyze sample-mean outcomes across subpopulations. We show that by constructing subpopulations that vary in expected human composition and treatment exposure, one can consistently recover human-specific causal effects. Our results characterize when distributional knowledge of population composition (without observing unit types or the interaction network) is sufficient for identification. We validate the approach on a simulated human-AI platform driven by behaviorally differentiated LLM agents. Together, these results provide a theoretical and practical framework for experimentation in emerging human-AI systems.

item.title: Pharmacology Knowledge Graphs: Do We Need Chemical Structure for Drug Repurposing?
item.content:encoded: The contributions of model complexity, data volume, and feature modalities to knowledge graph-based drug repurposing remain poorly quantified under rigorous temporal validation. We constructed a pharmacology knowledge graph from ChEMBL 36 comprising 5,348 entities including 3,127 drugs, 1,156 proteins, and 1,065 indications. A strict temporal split was enforced with training data up to 2022 and testing data from 2023 to 2025, together with biologically verified hard negatives mined from failed assays and clinical trials. We benchmarked five knowledge graph embedding models and a standard graph neural network with 3.44 million parameters that incorporates drug chemical structure using a graph attention encoder and ESM-2 protein embeddings. Scaling experiments ranging from 0.78 to 9.75 million parameters and from 25 to 100 percent of the data, together with feature ablation studies, were used to isolate the contributions of model capacity, graph density, and node feature modalities. Removing the graph attention based drug structure encoder and retaining only topological embeddings combined with ESM-2 protein features improved drug protein PR-AUC from 0.5631 to 0.5785 while reducing VRAM usage from 5.30 GB to 353 MB. Replacing the drug encoder with Morgan fingerprints further degraded performance, indicating that explicit chemical structure representations can be detrimental for predicting pharmacological network interactions. Increasing model size beyond 2.44 million parameters yielded diminishing returns, whereas increasing training data consistently improved performance. External validation confirmed 6 of the top 14 novel predictions as established therapeutic indications. These results show that drug pharmacological behavior can be accurately predicted using target-centric information and drug network topology alone, without requiring explicit chemical structure representations.

item.title: Adaptive Spectral Feature Forecasting for Diffusion Sampling Acceleration
item.content:encoded: Diffusion models have become the dominant tool for high-fidelity image and video generation, yet are critically bottlenecked by their inference speed due to the numerous iterative passes of Diffusion Transformers. To reduce the exhaustive compute, recent works resort to the feature caching and reusing scheme that skips network evaluations at selected diffusion steps by using cached features in previous steps. However, their preliminary design solely relies on local approximation, causing errors to grow rapidly with large skips and leading to degraded sample quality at high speedups. In this work, we propose spectral diffusion feature forecaster (Spectrum), a training-free approach that enables global, long-range feature reuse with tightly controlled error. In particular, we view the latent features of the denoiser as functions over time and approximate them with Chebyshev polynomials. Specifically, we fit the coefficient for each basis via ridge regression, which is then leveraged to forecast features at multiple future diffusion steps. We theoretically reveal that our approach admits more favorable long-horizon behavior and yields an error bound that does not compound with the step size. Extensive experiments on various state-of-the-art image and video diffusion models consistently verify the superiority of our approach. Notably, we achieve up to 4.79$\times$ speedup on FLUX.1 and 4.67$\times$ speedup on Wan2.1-14B, while maintaining much higher sample quality compared with the baselines.

item.title: Affine Correspondences in Stereo Vision: Theory, Practice, and Limitations
item.content:encoded: Affine transformations have been recently used for stereo vision. They can be exploited in various computer vision application, e.g., when estimating surface normals, homographies, fundamental and essential matrices. Even full 3D reconstruction can be obtained by using affine correspondences. First, this paper overviews the fundamental statements for affine transformations and epipolar geometry. Then it is investigated how the transformation accuracy influences the quality of the 3D reconstruction. Besides, we propose novel techniques for estimating the local affine transformation from corresponding image directions; moreover, the fundamental matrix, related to the processed image pair, can also be exploited. Both synthetic and real quantitative evaluations are implemented based on the accuracy of the reconstructed surface normals. For the latter one, a special object, containing three perpendicular planes with chessboard patterns, is constructed. The quantitative evaluations are based on the accuracy of the reconstructed surface normals and it is concluded that the estimation accuracy is around a few degrees for realistic test cases. Special stereo poses and plane orientations are also evaluated in detail.

item.title: LOCUS: A Distribution-Free Loss-Quantile Score for Risk-Aware Predictions
item.content:encoded: Modern machine learning models can be accurate on average yet still make mistakes that dominate deployment cost. We introduce Locus, a distribution-free wrapper that produces a per-input loss-scale reliability score for a fixed prediction function. Rather than quantifying uncertainty about the label, Locus models the realized loss of the prediction function using any engine that outputs a predictive distribution for the loss given an input. A simple split-calibration step turns this function into a distribution-free interpretable score that is comparable across inputs and can be read as an upper loss level. The score is useful on its own for ranking, and it can optionally be thresholded to obtain a transparent flagging rule with distribution-free control of large-loss events. Experiments across 13 regression benchmarks show that Locus yields effective risk ranking and reduces large-loss frequency compared to standard heuristics.

item.title: PanCanBench: A Comprehensive Benchmark for Evaluating Large Language Models in Pancreatic Oncology
item.content:encoded: Large language models (LLMs) have achieved expert-level performance on standardized examinations, yet multiple-choice accuracy poorly reflects real-world clinical utility and safety. As patients and clinicians increasingly use LLMs for guidance on complex conditions such as pancreatic cancer, evaluation must extend beyond general medical knowledge. Existing frameworks, such as HealthBench, rely on simulated queries and lack disease-specific depth. Moreover, high rubric-based scores do not ensure factual correctness, underscoring the need to assess hallucinations. We developed a human-in-the-loop pipeline to create expert rubrics for de-identified patient questions from the Pancreatic Cancer Action Network (PanCAN). The resulting benchmark, PanCanBench, includes 3,130 question-specific criteria across 282 authentic patient questions. We evaluated 22 proprietary and open-source LLMs using an LLM-as-a-judge framework, measuring clinical completeness, factual accuracy, and web-search integration. Models showed substantial variation in rubric-based completeness, with scores ranging from 46.5% to 82.3%. Factual errors were common, with hallucination rates (the percentages of responses containing at least one factual error) ranging from 6.0% for Gemini-2.5 Pro and GPT-4o to 53.8% for Llama-3.1-8B. Importantly, newer reasoning-optimized models did not consistently improve factuality: although o3 achieved the highest rubric score, it produced inaccuracies more frequently than other GPT-family models. Web-search integration did not inherently guarantee better responses. The average score changed from 66.8% to 63.9% for Gemini-2.5 Pro and from 73.8% to 72.8% for GPT-5 when web search was enabled. Synthetic AI-generated rubrics inflated absolute scores by 17.9 points on average while generally maintaining similar relative ranking.

item.title: MMR-Life: Piecing Together Real-life Scenes for Multimodal Multi-image Reasoning
item.content:encoded: Recent progress in the reasoning capabilities of multimodal large language models (MLLMs) has empowered them to address more complex tasks such as scientific analysis and mathematical reasoning. Despite their promise, MLLMs' reasoning abilities across different scenarios in real life remain largely unexplored and lack standardized benchmarks for evaluation. To address this gap, we introduce MMR-Life, a comprehensive benchmark designed to evaluate the diverse multimodal multi-image reasoning capabilities of MLLMs across real-life scenarios. MMR-Life consists of 2,646 multiple-choice questions based on 19,108 images primarily sourced from real-world contexts, comprehensively covering seven reasoning types: abductive, analogical, causal, deductive, inductive, spatial, and temporal. Unlike existing reasoning benchmarks, MMR-Life does not rely on domain-specific expertise but instead requires models to integrate information across multiple images and apply diverse reasoning abilities. The evaluation of 37 advanced models highlights the substantial challenge posed by MMR-Life. Even top models like GPT-5 achieve only 58% accuracy and display considerable variance in performance across reasoning types. Moreover, we analyze the reasoning paradigms of existing MLLMs, exploring how factors such as thinking length, reasoning method, and reasoning type affect their performance. In summary, MMR-Life establishes a comprehensive foundation for evaluating, analyzing, and improving the next generation of multimodal reasoning systems.

item.title: Toward Graph-Tokenizing Large Language Models with Reconstructive Graph Instruction Tuning
item.content:encoded: The remarkable success of large language models (LLMs) has motivated researchers to adapt them as universal predictors for various graph-related tasks, with the ultimate goal of developing a graph foundation model that generalizes diverse scenarios. The key challenge is to align graph data with language spaces so that LLMs can better comprehend graphs. As a popular paradigm, Graph-Tokenizing LLMs (GTokenLLMs) encode complex structures and lengthy texts into a graph token sequence, and then align them with text tokens via language instructions tuning. Despite their initial success, our information-theoretic analysis reveals that existing GTokenLLMs rely solely on text supervision from language instructions, which achieve only implicit graph-text alignment, resulting in a text-dominant bias that underutilizes graph context. To overcome this limitation, we first prove that the alignment objective is upper-bounded by the mutual information between the input graphs and their hidden representations in the LLM, which motivates us to improve this upper bound to achieve better alignment. To this end, we further propose a reconstructive graph instruction tuning pipeline, RGLM. Our key idea is to reconstruct the graph information from the LLM's graph token outputs, explicitly incorporating graph supervision to constrain the alignment process. Technically, we embody RGLM by exploring three distinct variants from two complementary perspectives: RGLM-Decoder from the input space; RGLM-Similarizer and RGLM-Denoiser from the latent space. Additionally, we theoretically analyze the alignment effectiveness of each variant. Extensive experiments on various benchmarks and task scenarios validate the effectiveness of the proposed RGLM, paving the way for new directions in GTokenLLMs' alignment research.

item.title: Anatomy of the Modality Gap: Dissecting the Internal States of End-to-End Speech LLMs
item.content:encoded: Recent advancements in Large Speech-Language Models have significantly bridged the gap between acoustic signals and linguistic understanding. However, a persistent performance disparity remains in speech-based input tasks compared to direct text inference. In this paper, we investigate the dynamic roots of this modality gap beyond static geometric alignment, analyzing how speech and text representations evolve layer-by-layer. We evaluate four open-weight end-to-end models on SpeechMMLU and VoiceBench BBH. Using cross-layer CKA analysis with speech-text token alignment, we find that speech representations exhibit a broad cross-layer alignment band, attributable to the redundant nature of speech where semantic content spans multiple frames. We show that these alignment patterns are structurally stable across different analysis configurations. Crucially, simple statistical calibration is insufficient and can be detrimental when applied at the input layer, indicating that the modality gap is not a mere distribution shift. Overall, our results suggest that the bottleneck lies in condensing redundant speech into stable late-layer decisions, motivating future solutions that operate at the token or temporal granularity instead of feature-level matching.

item.title: Assessing Crime Disclosure Patterns in a Large-Scale Cybercrime Forum
item.content:encoded: Cybercrime forums play a central role in the cybercrime ecosystem, serving as hubs for the exchange of illicit goods, services, and knowledge. Previous studies have explored the market and social structures of these forums, but less is known about the behavioral dynamics of users, particularly regarding participants' disclosure of criminal activity. This study provides the first large-scale assessment of crime disclosure patterns in a major cybercrime forum, analysing over 3.5 million posts from nearly 300k users. Using a three-level classification scheme (benign, grey, and crime) and a scalable labelling pipeline powered by large language models (LLMs), we measure the level of crime disclosure present in initial posts, analyse how participants switch between levels, and assess how crime disclosure behavior relates to private communications. Our results show that crime disclosure is relatively normative: one quarter of initial posts include explicit crime-related content, and more than one third of users disclose criminal activity at least once in their initial posts. At the same time, most participants show restraint, with over two-thirds posting only benign or grey content and typically escalating disclosure gradually. Grey initial posts are particularly prominent, indicating that many users avoid overt statements and instead anchor their activity in ambiguous content. The study highlights the value of LLM-based text classification and Markov chain modelling for capturing crime disclosure patterns, offering insights for law enforcement efforts aimed at distinguishing benign, grey, and criminal content in cybercrime forums.

item.title: MAP-Diff: Multi-Anchor Guided Diffusion for Progressive 3D Whole-Body Low-Dose PET Denoising
item.content:encoded: Low-dose Positron Emission Tomography (PET) reduces radiation exposure but suffers from severe noise and quantitative degradation. Diffusion-based denoising models achieve strong final reconstructions, yet their reverse trajectories are typically unconstrained and not aligned with the progressive nature of PET dose formation. We propose MAP-Diff, a multi-anchor guided diffusion framework for progressive 3D whole-body PET denoising. MAP-Diff introduces clinically observed intermediate-dose scans as trajectory anchors and enforces timestep-dependent supervision to regularize the reverse process toward dose-aligned intermediate states. Anchor timesteps are calibrated via degradation matching between simulated diffusion corruption and real multi-dose PET pairs, and a timestep-weighted anchor loss stabilizes stage-wise learning. At inference, the model requires only ultra-low-dose input while enabling progressive, dose-consistent intermediate restoration. Experiments on internal (Siemens Biograph Vision Quadra) and cross-scanner (United Imaging uEXPLORER) datasets show consistent improvements over strong CNN-, Transformer-, GAN-, and diffusion-based baselines. On the internal dataset, MAP-Diff improves PSNR from 42.48 dB to 43.71 dB (+1.23 dB), increases SSIM to 0.986, and reduces NMAE from 0.115 to 0.103 (-0.012) compared to 3D DDPM. Performance gains generalize across scanners, achieving 34.42 dB PSNR and 0.141 NMAE on the external cohort, outperforming all competing methods.

item.title: Solving Inverse PDE Problems using Minimization Methods and AI
item.content:encoded: Many physical and engineering systems require solving direct problems to predict behavior and inverse problems to determine unknown parameters from measurement. In this work, we study both aspects for systems governed by differential equations, contrasting well-established numerical methods with new AI-based techniques, specifically Physics-Informed Neural Networks (PINNs). We first analyze the logistic differential equation, using its closed-form solution to verify numerical schemes and validate PINN performance. We then address the Porous Medium Equation (PME), a nonlinear partial differential equation with no general closed-form solution, building strong solvers of the direct problem and testing techniques for parameter estimation in the inverse problem. Our results suggest that PINNs can closely estimate solutions at competitive computational cost, and thus propose an effective tool for solving both direct and inverse problems for complex systems.

item.title: A SUPERB-Style Benchmark of Self-Supervised Speech Models for Audio Deepfake Detection
item.content:encoded: Self-supervised learning (SSL) has transformed speech processing, with benchmarks such as SUPERB establishing fair comparisons across diverse downstream tasks. Despite it's security-critical importance, Audio deepfake detection has remained outside these efforts. In this work, we introduce Spoof-SUPERB, a benchmark for audio deepfake detection that systematically evaluates 20 SSL models spanning generative, discriminative, and spectrogram-based architectures. We evaluated these models on multiple in-domain and out-of-domain datasets. Our results reveal that large-scale discriminative models such as XLS-R, UniSpeech-SAT, and WavLM Large consistently outperform other models, benefiting from multilingual pretraining, speaker-aware objectives, and model scale. We further analyze the robustness of these models under acoustic degradations, showing that generative approaches degrade sharply, while discriminative models remain resilient. This benchmark establishes a reproducible baseline and provides practical insights into which SSL representations are most reliable for securing speech systems against audio deepfakes.

item.title: Co-Evolutionary Multi-Modal Alignment via Structured Adversarial Evolution
item.content:encoded: Adversarial behavior plays a central role in aligning large language models with human values. However, existing alignment methods largely rely on static adversarial settings, which fundamentally limit robustness, particularly in multimodal settings with a larger attack surface. In this work, we move beyond static adversarial supervision and introduce co-evolutionary alignment with evolving attacks, instantiated by CEMMA (Co-Evolutionary Multi-Modal Alignment), an automated and adaptive framework for multimodal safety alignment. We introduce an Evolutionary Attacker that decomposes adversarial prompts into method templates and harmful intents. By employing genetic operators, including mutation, crossover, and differential evolution, it enables simple seed attacks to inherit the structural efficacy of sophisticated jailbreaks. The Adaptive Defender is iteratively updated on the synthesized hard negatives, forming a closed-loop process that adapts alignment to evolving attacks. Experiments show that the Evolutionary Attacker substantially increases red-teaming jailbreak attack success rate (ASR), while the Adaptive Defender improves robustness and generalization across benchmarks with higher data efficiency, without inducing excessive benign refusal, and remains compatible with inference-time defenses such as AdaShield.

item.title: 提升视觉语言模型空间能力的视觉空间调优方法
item.content:encoded:
VST框架提升VLM的空间感知与推理能力。



摘要： 本文提出一种名为视觉空间调优（VST）的框架，旨在增强视觉语言模型（VLM）的空间感知与推理能力。通过构建大规模数据集VST-P和VST-R，分别用于提升空间感知和空间推理能力，并采用渐进式训练策略，包括监督微调和强化学习。实验结果表明，该方法在多个空间基准测试中取得最佳成绩，且不影响模型的整体能力，为更贴近物理世界的AI提供了新方向。

item.title: 3D人体动作与语言融合的新进展：密集动作描述与CompMo数据集
item.content:encoded:
提出CompMo数据集和DEMO模型，提升3D动作理解与描述能力。



摘要： 本文介绍了Dense Motion Captioning任务，旨在对3D人体动作序列进行时间定位和描述。由于现有数据集在时间标注和动作复杂度方面存在不足，作者提出了CompMo数据集，包含60,000个具有精确时间边界的动作序列。同时，开发了DEMO模型，结合大语言模型和运动适配器，实现密集且时间精准的描述生成。实验表明，DEMO在CompMo及适配基准上均表现优异，为3D动作理解和描述研究提供了坚实基础。

item.title: 构建具有工具调用能力的多模态代理模型
item.content:encoded:
研究提出DeepEyesV2，提升多模态模型的工具调用与推理能力。



摘要： 本文介绍了DeepEyesV2，探讨如何从数据构建、训练方法和模型评估角度构建具有工具调用能力的多模态代理模型。研究发现，仅靠强化学习难以实现稳定的工具使用行为，因此提出了两阶段训练流程：冷启动阶段建立工具使用模式，强化学习阶段进一步优化工具调用。文章还构建了一个多样且适度挑战的训练数据集，并引入RealX-Bench基准来评估真实世界的多模态推理能力。实验表明，DeepEyesV2在现实理解、数学推理和搜索密集型任务中表现出色，并能根据任务自适应调用图像处理或数值计算工具，强化学习还促进了复杂工具组合的使用。

item.title: 改进视觉语言模型中语言模态偏差的方法研究
item.content:encoded:
通过融合视觉特征提升文本嵌入，减少模型幻觉。



摘要： 本文指出当前视觉语言模型（LVLM）在语言模态上存在固有偏差，主要由于将视觉嵌入简单地拼接到文本序列中。为解决这一问题，作者提出一种简单有效的方法，通过整合平均池化后的视觉特征来优化文本嵌入。实验表明，该方法显著提升了视觉定位能力并减少了幻觉现象。尽管平均池化是一种简单、稳健且高效的视觉信息融合方式，但作者认为更复杂的融合策略可能进一步提升模型性能。本文主要关注模态不平衡及其对幻觉的影响，并展示了通过视觉信息优化文本嵌入可缓解该问题。

item.title: 大型语言模型在道德角色扮演中的表现研究
item.content:encoded:
LLMs在扮演反社会角色时表现不佳，安全对齐影响角色真实性。



摘要： 本文探讨了大型语言模型在扮演非亲社会角色时的局限性。研究发现，由于模型的安全对齐机制，它们在扮演道德模糊或邪恶角色时表现出显著的不一致性。作者构建了一个包含四个道德等级的基准数据集，并测试了最先进的LLMs在不同道德角色中的表现。结果显示，随着角色道德水平下降，角色扮演的真实性显著降低，尤其是面对与安全原则直接冲突的特质时，模型倾向于用表面攻击性代替复杂的恶意行为。研究还表明，通用聊天能力并不能有效预测角色扮演能力，尤其在高度安全对齐的模型中表现较差。该研究揭示了模型安全性与创造性表达之间的关键矛盾，并为开发更精细的对齐方法提供了方向。

item.title: 长上下文语言模型的安全漏洞研究
item.content:encoded:
NINJA方法利用长上下文提升攻击成功率。



摘要： 本文介绍了NINJA（Needle-in-haystack jailbreak attack）方法，该方法通过在有害用户目标中添加良性生成内容来突破对齐语言模型的安全限制。实验表明，NINJA在多个主流模型上显著提高了攻击成功率，且具有低资源消耗、可迁移性和隐蔽性。研究还发现，在固定计算预算下，增加上下文长度比增加尝试次数更有效。这揭示了即使是有益的长上下文，若目标位置设计得当，也可能成为现代语言模型的根本漏洞。

item.title: EVTAR：一种基于额外参考图像的端到端虚拟试衣模型
item.content:encoded:
EVTAR通过参考图像提升虚拟试衣精度，简化输入流程。



摘要： 本文提出EVTAR，一种端到端的虚拟试衣模型，利用额外参考图像来提高试衣效果的准确性。与以往依赖复杂输入（如人体姿态、密集姿势图等）的方法不同，EVTAR采用两阶段训练策略，在推理时仅需源图像和目标服装图像即可生成高质量的试衣结果。该模型无需使用掩码、密集姿势图或分割图，同时借助不同个体穿着相同衣物的参考图像，更好地保留服装纹理和细节。这种机制模拟了人类在挑选服装时参考他人穿搭的行为，提升了试衣的真实性和质量。实验表明，EVTAR在多个基准数据集和任务中均表现出色。

item.title: 基于3D模拟器的视频数据生成框架提升多模态语言模型的空间推理能力
item.content:encoded:
SIMS-V框架通过3D模拟生成高质量视频数据，提升多模态模型空间推理能力。



摘要： 本文提出SIMS-V框架，利用3D模拟器生成丰富的视频训练数据，以解决多模态语言模型在空间推理方面的不足。研究发现，三种关键问题类型（度量测量、视角依赖推理和时间跟踪）对现实世界任务的迁移效果最佳。实验表明，仅使用2.5万条模拟数据微调的7B参数模型，在多个真实场景基准测试中表现优于更大的72B模型，并达到与专有模型相当的水平。该方法在保持视频理解能力的同时，显著提升了空间任务的表现。

item.title: SAIL-RL：提升多模态大语言模型推理能力的强化学习框架
item.content:encoded:
SAIL-RL通过双重奖励机制提升多模态模型推理能力。



摘要： 本文介绍了SAIL-RL，一种用于增强多模态大语言模型（MLLMs）推理能力的强化学习后训练框架。传统方法受限于仅基于结果的监督，无法确保合理的推理过程。SAIL-RL引入了两种奖励机制：思考奖励评估推理质量，判断奖励决定是否需要深度推理或直接回答。实验表明，SAIL-RL在多个基准测试中表现优异，显著减少了幻觉现象，展现出与商业模型相当的性能。

item.title: 检测污染视觉语言模型的新方法研究
item.content:encoded:
提出一种基于多模态语义扰动的VLM污染检测方法。



摘要： 本文针对视觉语言模型（VLMs）在大规模预训练数据中可能存在的测试集泄露问题，提出了一个新颖的检测方法。通过故意污染开源VLMs并在多个基准测试中验证，发现现有检测方法效果不佳。作者提出基于多模态语义扰动的检测策略，并证明受污染模型在受控扰动下无法良好泛化。实验结果表明该方法在多种污染场景下均具有鲁棒性和有效性，相关代码和数据集将公开发布。

item.title: 源感知语音翻译评估方法研究
item.content:encoded:
研究源感知评估方法提升语音翻译系统评价准确性。



摘要： 本文首次系统研究了适用于语音翻译（ST）的源感知评估方法，尤其关注在无源文本情况下如何生成有效的文本代理。通过对比自动语音识别（ASR）转录和参考翻译的反向翻译两种策略，并引入一种新的跨语言重新分段算法来解决合成源与参考翻译之间的对齐问题。实验结果表明，在词错误率低于20%时，ASR转录比反向翻译更可靠，而反向翻译则更具计算效率。该研究为更准确、系统的语音翻译评估提供了新方向。

item.title: TransferEngine：实现跨硬件的高效点对点通信系统
item.content:encoded:
TransferEngine支持多NIC统一通信，提升LLM系统性能。



摘要： 文章介绍了TransferEngine，一种用于新兴大型语言模型系统的高效点对点通信框架。该系统支持 disaggregated inference、Mixture-of-Experts 路由和异步强化微调等模式，通过统一接口适配多种网络接口控制器（NIC），无需依赖特定硬件。TransferEngine利用单边写入和完成通知机制，提升了通信效率，并在NVIDIA ConnectX-7和AWS EFA上实现了400 Gbps的峰值吞吐量。文章展示了其在三个生产系统中的应用，包括动态缩放的KvCache传输、万亿参数模型的快速强化学习权重更新以及MoE调度与合并的高效实现，证明了其在提升模型性能和跨平台兼容性方面的价值。

item.title: 推动多模态智能发展的空间超感知范式与VSI-SUPER基准
item.content:encoded:
文章提出空间超感知框架，构建VSI-SUPER基准以推动多模态智能发展。



摘要： 文章主张真正的多模态智能应从反应性任务系统转向更广泛的超感知范式。作者将空间超感知分为四个阶段：语义感知、流式事件认知、隐式3D空间认知和预测世界建模。当前评估标准主要测试早期阶段，未能充分挑战模型的真实世界建模能力。为此，文章提出了VSI-SUPER基准，包含VSR和VSC两个任务，要求处理长视频输入并抵抗盲目扩展上下文。通过构建VSI-590K数据集和训练Cambrian-S模型，在VSI-Bench上取得显著提升。然而，VSI-SUPER上的表现仍有限，表明规模不足以实现空间超感知。文章进一步提出预测感知方法，并展示其在VSI-SUPER上的优越表现，强调多模态智能需具备预见、选择与组织经验的能力。

item.title: 多模态大模型基准测试的诊断与去偏方法研究
item.content:encoded:
研究揭示多模态模型可能依赖语言偏见而非视觉理解，提出去偏方法提升基准有效性。



摘要： 本文指出当前多模态大语言模型（MLLMs）在评估中可能依赖语言偏见和表面模式，而非真正的视觉理解。为解决这一问题，作者提出了一种诊断原则：如果基准可以被利用，它就会被利用。文章介绍了两种方法——“测试集压力测试”（TsT）和“迭代偏见修剪”（IBP），用于检测并去除非视觉偏见。通过在多个基准上应用这些方法，研究发现广泛存在的非视觉偏差，并以VSI-Bench-Debiased为例展示去偏后的效果。该研究有助于提升多模态模型评估的准确性和公平性。

item.title: 视频生成模型推动多模态推理新范式
item.content:encoded:
视频生成模型提升多模态推理能力。



摘要： 文章提出“Thinking with Video”新范式，利用视频生成模型如Sora-2，在统一的时间框架下增强视觉与文本的推理能力。研究构建了VideoThinkBench基准测试，涵盖视觉和文本任务，评估结果显示Sora-2在多项任务中表现优异，甚至超越现有先进模型。同时发现自洽性和上下文学习可进一步提升其性能，表明视频生成模型具有成为统一多模态理解与生成模型的潜力。

item.title: V-Thinker：一种基于视觉交互的多模态推理框架
item.content:encoded:
V-Thinker提升多模态模型的视觉交互推理能力。



摘要： 本文提出V-Thinker，一种通用的多模态推理助手，通过端到端强化学习实现视觉驱动的深度交互推理。该框架包含数据演化飞轮和视觉渐进式训练课程，能够自动合成、进化并验证交互推理数据集，并通过点级监督和两阶段强化学习提升模型的视觉理解与推理能力。此外，作者还构建了VTBench基准测试，用于评估视觉交互推理任务。实验表明，V-Thinker在通用和交互推理场景中均优于现有基线模型，为图像交互推理应用提供了新思路。

item.title: GUI-360^circ数据集与计算机使用代理研究
item.content:encoded:
GUI-360^circ推动计算机使用代理研究。



摘要： GUI-360^circ是一个大规模、全面的数据集和基准套件，旨在推进计算机使用代理（CUAs）的发展。该数据集通过LLM增强的自动化流程解决了真实任务稀缺、多模态轨迹标注不足以及缺乏统一评估基准的问题。数据集包含超过120万次操作步骤，涵盖多个Windows办公应用的轨迹，并支持GUI接地、屏幕解析和动作预测等任务。实验表明，现有模型在接地和动作预测方面仍有显著不足，尽管经过微调和强化学习有所提升，但尚未达到人类水平。数据集已公开以促进可重复研究。

item.title: 多头注意力机制中的强彩票票根理论分析
item.content:encoded:
研究多头注意力中强彩票票根的存在性及近似效果。



摘要： 本文探讨了多头注意力（MHA）机制中强彩票票根（SLT）的存在性，并证明了在特定隐藏维度下，随机初始化的MHA能够以高概率近似任意相同输入维度的MHA。此外，基于该理论，研究将强彩票票根假设扩展到无归一化层的Transformer架构。实验验证表明，源模型与目标模型之间的近似误差随着隐藏维度增加而指数级下降。

item.title: 基于强化学习的人形机器人足球控制方法
item.content:encoded:
人形机器人足球控制实现感知与动作的统一整合。



摘要： 本文提出一种基于强化学习的统一控制器，使双足机器人能够在动态环境中通过直接整合视觉感知和运动控制来获得反应式的足球技能。该方法扩展了对抗性运动先验到现实动态环境中的感知设置，结合编码器-解码器结构与虚拟感知系统，模拟真实视觉特征，使策略能够从不完整的观测中恢复特权状态，并建立感知与动作之间的主动协调。该控制器在多种场景下表现出强反应性与一致性，成功应用于实际RoboCup比赛。

item.title: Nemotron Nano V2 VL：提升文档与视频理解的视觉语言模型
item.content:encoded:
Nemotron Nano V2 VL在文档和视频理解方面有显著提升。



摘要： Nemotron Nano V2 VL是Nemotron视觉语言系列的最新模型，专为现实世界的文档理解和长视频推理任务设计。相比之前的Llama-3.1-Nemotron-Nano-VL-8B模型，它在所有视觉和文本领域都有显著提升，得益于模型架构、数据集和训练方法的重大改进。该模型基于Nemotron Nano V2，结合Mamba-Transformer混合架构和创新的令牌缩减技术，提高了长文档和视频场景下的推理吞吐量。官方提供了BF16、FP8和FP4格式的模型检查点，并分享了部分数据集、训练配方和代码。

item.title: DreamGym：一种用于强化学习的统一经验合成框架
item.content:encoded:
DreamGym通过合成经验提升强化学习训练效果。



摘要： 本文介绍了DreamGym，这是一个旨在解决大规模语言模型在强化学习中面临挑战的统一框架。DreamGym通过推理驱动的经验模型生成多样且可扩展的体验数据，从而减少对昂贵真实环境交互的依赖。它利用经验回放缓冲区和自适应任务生成机制，提高训练稳定性和知识获取效率。实验表明，DreamGym在多种环境中显著提升了强化学习性能，尤其在模拟到现实的迁移任务中表现突出，展示了其在实际应用中的潜力。

item.title: 基于视角主义标注的协作对话理解研究
item.content:encoded:
研究协作对话中的共同理解机制及误解现象。



摘要： 本文针对协作对话中参与者因视角差异导致的理解偏差问题，提出了一种基于视角主义的标注方案，对HCRC MapTask语料库进行标注，以追踪对话过程中理解的形成、分歧与修复过程。通过约束条件下的LLM标注流程，获取了13,000个参考表达的标注数据，并分析了不同理解状态。研究发现，统一词汇变体后完全误解较少，但多义性差异常引发分歧，揭示了表面共识下可能存在的指代不一致。该框架为研究具身化误解和评估（V）LLMs在协作对话中的视角建模能力提供了资源与分析工具。

item.title: 顺序推理优于并行推理：语言模型推理的测试时扩展再评估
item.content:encoded:
顺序推理在多数配置中优于并行推理，提升准确率高达46.7%。



摘要： 本文重新评估了语言模型推理中的测试时扩展方法，探讨在相同计算和token预算下，是选择并行运行多个独立推理链，还是选择顺序迭代优化的推理链。通过在5个先进的开源模型和3个挑战性推理基准上的全面评估，研究发现顺序扩展方法在95.6%的配置中表现更优，最高准确率提升达46.7%。此外，研究引入了一种无需训练的逆熵加权投票方法，进一步提升了顺序扩展的准确性。该方法根据推理链的逆熵对答案进行加权，提高了成功率，并确立其为最优测试时扩展策略。研究结果挑战了自2022年以来主导测试时扩展的并行推理范式，表明顺序优化应成为现代大语言模型推理的默认策略。

item.title: UniAVGen：统一的音视频生成框架提升同步与语义一致性
item.content:encoded:
UniAVGen提升音视频同步与语义一致性。



摘要： 本文提出UniAVGen，一个统一的音视频生成框架，旨在解决现有方法在唇形同步和语义一致性方面的不足。该框架采用双分支联合合成架构，结合两个并行的Diffusion Transformers（DiTs）构建跨模态潜在空间，并引入非对称跨模态交互机制，实现双向、时间对齐的跨注意力，确保精确的时空同步和语义一致性。此外，还引入了面向面部的调制模块和模态感知的无分类器引导策略，以增强生成质量。实验表明，UniAVGen在少量训练样本下表现出色，优于现有方法。

item.title: 扩散语言模型在有限数据下的性能超越自回归模型
item.content:encoded:
扩散语言模型在数据有限时表现优于自回归模型。



摘要： 研究发现，在严格控制的预训练条件下，当可用数据有限时，扩散语言模型（DLMs）通过更多轮次的训练能够持续超越自回归（AR）模型。这种性能优势随着数据量或质量的增加而延迟显现，但随着模型规模增大则更早出现。研究认为这种提升主要源于三个因素：任意顺序建模、迭代双向去噪带来的超密集计算以及内置的蒙特卡洛增强。在大规模实验中，一个17亿参数的DLM在100亿个Python唯一token上训练后，超过了相同设置下的AR编码器。此外，一个10亿参数的DLM仅通过重复标准预训练数据即可在HellaSwag和MMLU任务中取得优异成绩。研究还表明，在这种情况下，验证交叉熵上升并不意味着下游任务性能下降。

item.title: Orion-MSP：一种高效处理表格数据的上下文学习架构
item.content:encoded:
Orion-MSP提升表格数据处理效率与性能。



摘要： 文章介绍了一种名为Orion-MSP的表格数据上下文学习（ICL）架构，旨在解决传统方法在处理异构特征和多尺度交互时的不足。该模型引入了三个关键创新：多尺度特征处理、块稀疏注意力机制以及类似Perceiver的记忆结构，从而提升了模型在高维表格上的表现和计算效率。实验结果表明，Orion-MSP在多个基准测试中达到或超越现有最佳性能，为高效表格数据学习提供了新标准。

item.title: TabTune：统一表格基础模型的标准化框架
item.content:encoded:
TabTune统一了表格基础模型的预训练与微调流程。



摘要： 文章介绍了TabTune，一个用于表格基础模型的统一库，旨在解决预处理流程不一致、API碎片化和缺乏标准化评估等问题。该框架支持多种适应策略，如零样本推理、元学习、监督微调和参数高效微调，并提供自动化的模型感知预处理、架构异构性管理以及性能、校准和公平性的评估模块。TabTune强调可扩展性和可重复性，便于对表格基础模型的适应策略进行基准测试，且为开源项目。

item.title: LiveTradeBench：评估大语言模型在动态市场中决策能力的新基准
item.content:encoded:
LiveTradeBench测试LLM在真实市场中的交易表现。



摘要： 本文介绍了LiveTradeBench，一个用于评估大语言模型（LLMs）在动态、实时市场环境中进行投资决策能力的基准测试平台。该平台通过实时数据流、多资产组合管理和跨市场评估，模拟真实市场的不确定性与复杂性。实验表明，高评分的LLM并不一定在交易中表现优异，不同模型展现出不同的风险偏好和决策风格，并且部分模型能有效利用实时信息调整策略。研究揭示了静态评测与实际应用之间的差距，强调了对动态决策能力进行评估的重要性。

item.title: 多模态大模型认知能力评估基准MME-CC的构建与分析
item.content:encoded:
研究构建了MME-CC基准，评估多模态大模型的认知能力。



摘要： 随着多模态大模型的快速发展，视觉为中心的认知行为成为研究重点。现有基准在文本推理和视觉行为捕捉上存在不足，为此，研究者提出了MME-CC基准，涵盖空间、几何和知识推理三类任务，对16个主流模型进行评估。结果显示，闭源模型表现优于开源模型，但空间和几何推理仍较弱，存在方向错误、跨视角身份不一致等问题。研究揭示了链式推理的三阶段过程，并强调视觉信息提取的重要性，旨在推动模型设计更加关注认知能力。

item.title: 提升3D场景生成质量：基于细粒度指令的评估框架与基准测试
item.content:encoded:
研究提出LEGO-Eval评估框架，提升3D场景与细粒度指令的对齐度。



摘要： 尽管大型语言模型在自动生成3D场景方面取得进展，但生成的场景常缺乏真实世界的空间布局和物体属性。这一问题源于指令过于粗略。为解决此问题，研究引入了LEGO-Eval评估框架，通过多种工具更准确地评估场景与指令的对齐度，并提出了LEGO-Bench基准测试，用于评估复杂场景生成能力。实验表明，LEGO-Eval在评估对齐度上优于现有方法，而当前生成技术在完全符合细粒度指令的场景生成中成功率不足10%。

item.title: M-Solomon：一种自适应查询增强的多模态嵌入方法
item.content:encoded:
M-Solomon通过自适应查询增强提升多模态检索效果。



摘要： 本文提出M-Solomon，一种自适应查询增强的多模态嵌入方法。该方法在训练阶段将查询分为需要增强和不需要增强两类，并利用多模态大语言模型生成合适的增强内容。在推理阶段，M-Solomon仅在必要时进行查询增强，从而减少嵌入延迟并提升性能。实验结果表明，M-Solomon在多个基准上优于不进行增强和始终进行增强的基线模型。
